   FROM PlayerGameStatsCTE¥n    GROUP BY team_id, team_name¥n),¥nCumulativeStatsCTE AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        player_name,¥n        team_name,¥n        date,¥n        location,¥n        goals,¥n        assists,¥n        penalty_minutes,¥n        plus_minus,¥n        time_on_ice,¥n        faceoff_wins,¥n        faceoff_losses,¥n        hits,¥n        blocked_shots,¥n        giveaways,¥n        takeaways,¥n        shots_on_goal,¥n        power_play_goals,¥n        power_play_assists,¥n        short_handed_goals,¥n        short_handed_assists,¥n        SUM(goals) OVER (PARTITION BY team_name, player_name ORDER BY date) AS cumulative_goals,¥n        SUM(assists) OVER (PARTITION BY team_name, player_name ORDER BY date) AS cumulative_assists,¥n        SUM(shots_on_goal) OVER (PARTITION BY team_name, player_name ORDER BY date) AS cumulative_shots,¥n        COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY team_name, player_name ORDER BY date) AS cumulative_games¥n    FROM PlayerGameStatsCTE¥n),¥nFinalRankingCTE AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        player_name,¥n        team_name,¥n        date,¥n        location,¥n        goals,¥n        assists,¥n        penalty_minutes,¥n        plus_minus,¥n        time_on_ice,¥n        faceoff_wins,¥n        faceoff_losses,¥n        hits,¥n        blocked_shots,¥n        giveaways,¥n        takeaways,¥n        shots_on_goal,¥n        power_play_goals,¥n        power_play_assists,¥n        short_handed_goals,¥n        short_handed_assists,¥n        cumulative_goals,¥n        cumulative_assists,¥n        cumulative_shots,¥n        cumulative_games,¥n        DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY team_name ORDER BY cumulative_goals DESC, cumulative_assists DESC) AS ranking¥n    FROM CumulativeStatsCTE¥n),¥nUserRolesCTE AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        u.user_name,¥n        ur.role_name¥n    FROM users u¥n    JOIN user_roles ur ON u.role_id = ur.role_id¥n)¥nSELECT ¥n    fr.player_name,¥n    fr.team_name,¥n    fr.date,¥n    fr.location,¥n    fr.goals,¥n    fr.assists,¥n    fr.penalty_minutes,¥n    fr.plus_minus,¥n    fr.time_on_ice,¥n    fr.faceoff_wins,¥n    fr.faceoff_losses,¥n    fr.hits,¥n    fr.blocked_shots,¥n    fr.giveaways,¥n    fr.takeaways,¥n    fr.shots_on_goal,¥n    fr.power_play_goals,¥n    fr.power_play_assists,¥n    fr.short_handed_goals,¥n    fr.short_handed_assists,¥n    fr.cumulative_goals,¥n    fr.cumulative_assists,¥n    fr.cumulative_shots,¥n    fr.cumulative_games,¥n    fr.ranking,¥n    ur.user_name AS analyst_name,¥n    ur.role_name AS role_name¥nFROM FinalRankingCTE fr¥nJOIN UserRolesCTE ur ON ur.role_name = 'analyst'¥nWHERE fr.ranking = 1;¥n```¥n¥n### Explanation:¥n- **PlayerGameStatsCTE**: Joins necessary tables to get detailed player statistics.¥n- **TeamPerformanceCTE**: Calculates total performance metrics for each team.¥n- **CumulativeStatsCTE**: Computes cumulative statistics for each player over time.¥n- **FinalRankingCTE**: Ranks players within each team based on cumulative goals and assists.¥n- **UserRolesCTE**: Fetches analyst names and roles.¥n- **Final SELECT**: Joins `FinalRankingCTE` with `UserRolesCTE` and filters for top-ranked players (ranking = 1).¥n¥nThis query should provide the required information about top-ranked players in each team, their detailed statistics, performance over time, and the names and roles of the analysts.

g.location,¥n        pgs.goals,¥n        pgs.assists,¥n        pgs.penalty_minutes,¥n        pgs.plus_minus,¥n        pgs.time_on_ice,¥n        pgs.faceoff_wins,¥n        pgs.faceoff_losses,¥n pgs.short_handed_goals,¥n        pgs.short_handed_assists¥n    FROM player_game_stats pgs¥n    JOIN players p ON pgs.player_id = p.player_id¥n    JOIN teams t ON p.team_id = t.team_id¥n    JOIN games g ON pgs.game_id = g.game_id¥n),¥nTeamPerformanceCTE AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        team_id,¥n        team_name,¥n        SUM(goals) AS total_goals,¥n        SUM(assists) AS total_assists,¥n        SUM(shots_on_goal) AS total_shots,¥n        COUNT(*) AS total_games¥n    FROM PlayerGameStatsCTE¥n    GROUP BY team_id, team_name¥n),¥nCumulativeStatsCTE AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        player_name,¥n        team_name,¥n        date,¥n        location,¥n        goals,¥n        assists,¥n        penalty_minutes,¥n        plus_minus,¥n        time_on_ice,¥n        faceoff_wins,¥n        faceoff_losses,¥n AS cumulative_assists,¥n        SUM(shots_on_goal) OVER (PARTITION BY team_name, player_name ORDER BY date) AS cumulative_shots,¥n        COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY team_name, player_name ORDER BY date) AS cumulative_games¥n    FROM PlayerGameStatsCTE¥n),¥nFinalRankingCTE AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        player_name,¥n        team_name,¥n        date,¥n        location,¥n        goals,¥n        assists,¥n        penalty_minutes,¥n        plus_minus,¥n        time_on_ice,¥n        faceoff_wins,¥n        faceoff_losses,¥n        hits,¥n        blocked_shots,¥n        giveaways,¥n        takeaways,¥n        shots_on_goal,¥n        power_play_goals,¥n        power_play_assists,¥n        short_handed_goals,¥n        short_handed_assists,¥n        cumulative_goals,¥n        cumulative_assists,¥n        cumulative_shots,¥n        cumulative_games,¥n        DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY team_name ORDER BY cumulative_goals DESC, cumulative_assists DESC) AS ranking¥n    FROM CumulativeStatsCTE¥n),¥nUserRolesCTE AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        u.user_name,¥n        ur.role_name¥n    FROM users u¥n    JOIN user_roles ur ON u.role_id = ur.role_id¥n)¥nSELECT ¥n    fr.player_name,¥n    fr.team_name,¥n    fr.date,¥n    fr.location,¥n    fr.goals,¥n    fr.assists,¥n    fr.penalty_minutes,¥n    fr.plus_minus,¥n    fr.time_on_ice,¥n    fr.faceoff_wins,¥n    fr.faceoff_losses,¥n    fr.hits,¥n    fr.blocked_shots,¥n    fr.giveaways,¥n    fr.takeaways,¥n    fr.shots_on_goal,¥n    fr.power_play_goals,¥n    fr.power_play_assists,¥n    fr.short_handed_goals,¥n    fr.short_handed_assists,¥n    fr.cumulative_goals,¥n    fr.cumulative_assists,¥n    fr.cumulative_shots,¥n    fr.cumulative_games,¥n    fr.ranking,¥n    ur.user_name AS analyst_name,¥n    ur.role_name AS role_name¥nFROM FinalRankingCTE fr¥nJOIN UserRolesCTE ur ON ur.role_name = 'analyst'¥nWHERE fr.ranking = 1;

question: Can you please get the names of the top-performing players in each game, based on their total goals and assists? I need to know which player performed the best in each game.
cot: Let's walk through the process of translating the natural language question into an executable SQLite query.¥n¥n### Step 1: Understand the question¥nThe question asks for the names of the top-performing players in each game. ¥

is defined by the total goals and assists a player has scored in each game. We need to identify the player with the highest combined goals and assists for each game.¥n¥n### Step 2: Identify the relevant tables¥n- `player_game_stats`: This table contains the performance statistics for each player in each game, including goals and assists.¥n- `players`: This table contains the names of the players.¥n¥n### Step 3: Determine the necessary columns¥n- From `player_game_stats`, we need `player_id`, `game_id`, `goals`, and `assists`.¥n- From `players`, we need `player_name`.¥n¥n### Step 4: Calculate the total performance metric (goals + assists)¥nWe need to sum up the goals and assists for each player in each game. This will give us a single metric to rank the players based on their performance.¥n¥n### Step 5: Rank players within each game¥nWe will use the `RANK()` window function to rank players based on their total performance (goals + assists) within each game. The `PARTITION BY` clause will ensure that the ranking is reset for each game.¥n¥n### Step 6: Select the top-performing player for each game¥nAfter ranking, we will select the player with the highest rank (rank = 1) for each game.¥n¥n### Step 7: Join the results with the players table to get the player names¥nFinally, we will join the results with the `players` table to get the names of the top-performing players.¥n¥n### Step 8: Write the SQL query¥nBased on the above steps, here is the SQL query:¥n¥n```sql¥nWITH PlayerGameStatsCTE AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        player_id,¥n        game_id,¥n        SUM(goals) AS total_goals,¥n        SUM(assists) AS total_assists,¥n        RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY game_id ORDER BY SUM(goals + assists) DESC) AS performance_rank¥n    FROM ¥n        player_game_stats¥n    GROUP BY ¥n        player_id, game_id¥n),¥nTopPlayerPerGameCTE AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        game_id,¥n        player_id,¥n        total_goals,¥n        total_assists¥n    FROM ¥n        PlayerGameStatsCTE¥n    WHERE ¥n        performance_rank = 1¥n)¥nSELECT ¥n    tpg.game_id,¥n    pl.player_name¥nFROM ¥n    TopPlayerPerGameCTE tpg¥nINNER JOIN ¥n    players pl ON tpg.player_id = pl.player_id;¥n```¥n¥n### Explanation:¥n1. **PlayerGameStatsCTE**: This common table expression (CTE) calculates the total goals and assists for each player in each game and ranks them based on their performance.¥n2. **TopPlayerPerGameCTE**: This CTE selects the top-performing player for each game by filtering the results where the `performance_rank` is 1.¥n3. The final `SELECT` statement joins the `TopPlayerPerGameCTE` with the `players` table to get the names of the top-performing players.¥n¥nThis query efficiently identifies the top-performing player in each game based on their total goals and assists.
sql: WITH PlayerGameStatsCTE AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        player_id,¥n        game_id,¥n        SUM(goals) AS total_goals,¥n        SUM(assists) AS total_assists,¥n        RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY game_id ORDER BY SUM(goals + assists) DESC) AS performance_rank¥n    FROM ¥n        player_game_stats¥n    GROUP BY ¥n        player_id, game_id¥n),¥nTopPlayerPerGameCTE AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        game_id,¥n        player_id,¥n        total_goals,¥n        total_assists¥n    FROM ¥n        PlayerGameStatsCTE¥n    WHERE ¥n        performance_rank = 1¥n)¥nSELECT ¥n    tpg.game_id,¥n    pl.player_name¥nFROM ¥n    TopPlayerPerGameCTE tpg¥nINNER JOIN ¥n    players pl ON tpg.player_id = pl.player_id;

question: Find the top-performing players in the league, ranking them by their total goals and assists, and including their overall performance statistics such as games played, total penalty minutes, plus-minus rating, hits, blocked shots, shots on goal, and average time on ice.
external_knowledge: In the context of ice hockey, a player's performance is often evaluated based on their goals, assists, and other statistics. The ¥
refers to those who have scored the most goals and assists; ¥

encompass various aspects of a player's game, including their discipline (penalty minutes), impact on the game (plus-minus rating), physicality (hits and blocked shots), and shooting ability (shots on goal), as well as their average time spent on the ice during games.
cot: To answer this natural language question, we need to construct an SQL query that calculates the top-performing players based on their goals and assists, and includes their overall performance statistics. Here is the step-by-step reasoning to derive the SQL query:¥n¥n1. **Identify the Relevant Tables and Columns**:¥n   - We need the `player_game_stats` table to get the performance statistics (goals, assists, penalty minutes, plus-minus, hits, blocked shots, shots on goal, and time on ice).¥n   - We need the `players` table to get the player names.¥n¥n2. **Aggregate the Data**:¥n   - We need to aggregate the data by `player_id` and calculate the total goals, total assists, total penalty minutes, total plus-minus, total hits, total blocked shots, total shots on goal, and average time on ice.¥n   - We will use `COUNT(*)` to get the number of games played by each player.¥n   - We will use `SUM()` for goals, assists, penalty minutes, hits, and blocked shots.¥n   - We will use `SUM()` and then average for time on ice.¥n¥n3. **Rank the Players**:¥n   - We need to rank the players based on their total goals and assists in descending order.¥n   - We will use the `ROW_NUMBER()` window function to assign a rank to each player.¥n¥n4. **Select the Required Columns**:¥n   - We need to select the player name, games played, total goals, total assists, total penalty minutes, total plus-minus, total hits, total blocked shots, total shots on goal, average time on ice, and the player rank.¥n¥n5. **Order the Results**:¥n   - We need to order the results by the player rank in ascending order to get the top-performing players at the top.¥n¥nHere is the final SQL query:¥n¥n```sql¥nWITH PlayerStatsCTE AS (¥n    SELECT¥n        pgs.player_id,¥n        p.player_name,¥n        COUNT(*) AS games_played,¥n        SUM(pgs.goals) AS total_goals,¥n        SUM(pgs.assists) AS total_assists,¥n        SUM(pgs.penalty_minutes) AS total_penalty_minutes,¥n        SUM(pgs.plus_minus) AS total_plus_minus,¥n        SUM(pgs.hits) AS total_hits,¥n        SUM(pgs.blocked_shots) AS total_blocked_shots,¥n        SUM(pgs.shots_on_goal) AS total_shots_on_goal,¥n        AVG(pgs.time_on_ice) AS avg_time_on_ice¥n    FROM¥n        player_game_stats pgs¥n    INNER JOIN¥n        players p ON pgs.player_id = p.player_id¥n    GROUP BY¥n        pgs.player_id, p.player_name¥n),¥nRankedPlayerStats AS (¥n    SELECT¥n        player_id,¥n        player_name,¥n        games_played,¥n        total_goals,¥n        total_assists,¥n        total_penalty_minutes,¥n        total_plus_minus,¥n        total_hits,¥n        total_blocked_shots,¥n        total_shots_on_goal,¥n        avg_time_on_ice,¥n        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY total_goals DESC, total_assists DESC) AS player_rank¥n    FROM¥n        PlayerStatsCTE¥n)¥nSELECT¥n    player_name,¥n    games_played,¥n    total_goals,¥n    total_assists,¥n    total_penalty_minutes,¥n    total_plus_minus,¥n    total_hits,¥n    total_blocked_shots,¥n    total_shots_on_goal,¥n    avg_time_on_ice,¥n    player_rank¥nFROM¥n    RankedPlayerStats¥nORDER BY¥n    player_rank ASC;¥n```¥n¥nThis query will give us the top-performing players in the league, ranked by their total goals and assists, and including their overall performance statistics.
sql: WITH PlayerStatsCTE AS (¥n    SELECT¥n        pgs.player_id,¥n        p.player_name,¥n        COUNT(*) AS games_played,¥n        SUM(pgs.goals) AS total_goals,¥n        SUM(pgs.assists) AS total_assists,¥n        SUM(pgs.penalty_minutes) AS total_penalty_minutes,¥n        SUM(pgs.plus_minus) AS total_plus_minus,¥n        SUM(pgs.hits) AS total_hits,¥n        SUM(pgs.blocked_shots) AS total_blocked_shots,¥n        SUM(pgs.shots_on_goal) AS total_shots_on_goal,¥n        AVG(pgs.time_on_ice) AS avg_time_on_ice¥n    FROM¥n        player_game_stats pgs¥n    INNER JOIN¥n        players p ON pgs.player_id = p.player_id¥n    GROUP BY¥n        pgs.player_id, p.player_name¥n),¥nRankedPlayerStats AS (¥n    SELECT¥n        player_id,¥n        player_name,¥n        games_played,¥n        total_goals,¥n        total_assists,¥n        total_penalty_minutes,¥n        total_plus_minus,¥n        total_hits,¥n        total_blocked_shots,¥n        total_shots_on_goal,¥n        avg_time_on_ice,¥n        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY total_goals DESC, total_assists DESC) AS player_rank¥n    FROM¥n        PlayerStatsCTE¥n)¥nSELECT¥n    player_name,¥n    games_played,¥n    total_goals,¥n    total_assists,¥n    total_penalty_minutes,¥n    total_plus_minus,¥n    total_hits,¥n    total_blocked_shots,¥n    total_shots_on_goal,¥n    avg_time_on_ice,¥n    player_rank¥nFROM¥n    RankedPlayerStats¥nORDER BY¥n    player_rank ASC;

question: Can you please identify the top-performing player in each game, along with their activity score and faceoff wins? To determine the top performer, consider the player's total goals, assists, and penalty minutes, and in case of a tie, look at the player's faceoff wins. I need to know the game ID, player ID, activity score, and faceoff wins for each top performer.
cot: To address the natural language question, we need to identify the top-performing player in each game based on their activity score and faceoff wins. The activity score is calculated as the sum of the player's goals, assists, and penalty minutes. If two players have the same activity score, the player with more faceoff wins is considered the top performer.¥n¥nHere's the step-by-step breakdown of how to derive the SQL query:¥n¥n1. **Identify the necessary tables**: We need to access the `player_game_stats` table to get the player performance data, including goals, assists, penalty minutes, and faceoff wins. We also need the `game_id` to identify the game for each player's performance.¥n¥n2. **Calculate the activity score**: For each player in each game, we need to calculate the activity score as the sum of goals, assists, and penalty minutes.¥n¥n3. **Determine the top performer**: We need to rank the players within each game based on their activity score. If there is a tie in the activity score, we should use the faceoff wins to break the tie.¥n¥n4. **Select the top performer**: We need to select the player with the highest rank (i.e., the top performer) for each game.¥n¥n5. **Construct the SQL query**: We will use a Common Table Expression (CTE) to calculate the activity score and rank the players, and then select the top performer from the CTE.¥n¥nHere's the SQL query to achieve this:¥n¥n```sql¥nWITH PlayerStatsCTE AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        game_id, ¥n        player_id,¥n        SUM(goals + assists + penalty_minutes) AS activity_score,¥n        SUM(faceoff_wins) AS faceoff_wins,¥n        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY game_id ORDER BY SUM(goals + assists + penalty_minutes) DESC, SUM(faceoff_wins) DESC) AS rank¥n    FROM player_game_stats¥n    GROUP BY game_id, player_id¥n)¥nSELECT ¥n    pgs.game_id,¥n    pgs.player_id,¥n    pgs.activity_score,¥n    pgs.faceoff_wins¥nFROM PlayerStatsCTE pgs¥nWHERE pgs.rank = 1;¥n```¥n¥n### Explanation of the Query:¥n¥n- **PlayerStatsCTE**: This CTE calculates the activity score and faceoff wins for each player in each game. It also ranks the players within each game based on their activity score and faceoff wins.¥n¥n- **ROW_NUMBER()**: The `ROW_NUMBER()` window function is used to assign a rank to each player within each game. The ranking is done in descending order of the activity score and, in case of a tie, in descending order of faceoff wins.¥n¥n- **Main Query**: The main query selects the game ID, player ID, activity score, and faceoff wins for the top performer (i.e., the player with rank = 1) from the CTE.¥n¥nThis query will return the top-performing player in each game along with their activity score and faceoff wins.
sql: WITH PlayerStatsCTE AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        game_id, ¥n        player_id,¥n        SUM(goals + assists + penalty_minutes) AS activity_score,¥n        SUM(faceoff_wins) AS faceoff_wins,¥n        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY game_id ORDER BY SUM(goals + assists + penalty_minutes) DESC, SUM(faceoff_wins) DESC) AS rank¥n    FROM player_game_stats¥n    GROUP BY game_id, player_id¥n)¥nSELECT ¥n    pgs.game_id,¥n    pgs.player_id,¥n    pgs.activity_score,¥n    pgs.faceoff_wins¥nFROM PlayerStatsCTE pgs¥nWHERE pgs.rank = 1;

question: What are the averages of the number of goals scored per game in the 2010 season for games that have been played more than 10 times?
cot: Let's break down the task and translate the natural language question into an executable SQL query.¥n¥n### Step-by-Step Solution:¥n¥n1. **Understand the Question**:¥n   - We need to find the average number of goals scored per game in the 2010 season.¥n   - Additionally, we need to consider only those games that have been played more than 10 times.¥n¥n2. **Identify Relevant Tables**:¥n   - **player_game_stats**: Contains the number of goals scored by each player in each game.¥n   - **games**: Contains details about each game, including the season it belongs to.¥n   - **seasons**: Contains information about each season, including its name.¥n¥n3. **Formulate the Query**:¥n   - We need to select the average of the number of goals (`goals`) from the `player_game_stats` table.¥n   - We need to filter the records to include only those from the 2010 season.¥n   - We need to group the results by game ID (`game_id`) to calculate the average goals per game.¥n   - We need to apply a having clause to ensure that only games that have been played more than 10 times are considered.¥n¥n4. **Construct the SQL Query**:¥n   - **SELECT Clause**: We select the average of the `goals` column.¥n   - **FROM Clause**: We start from the `player_game_stats` table.¥n   - **INNER JOIN**: We join the `games` table to link the game IDs.¥n   - **INNER JOIN**: We join the `seasons` table to filter the records by the 2010 season.¥n   - **WHERE Clause**: We filter the records to include only those from the 2010 season.¥n   - **GROUP BY Clause**: We group the results by `game_id`.¥n   - **HAVING Clause**: We ensure that only games with more than 10 distinct game IDs are considered.¥n¥n### SQL Query:¥n¥n```sql¥nSELECT AVG(pg.goals) AS avg_goals_per_game¥nFROM player_game_stats pg¥nINNER JOIN games g ON pg.game_id = g.game_id¥nINNER JOIN seasons s ON g.season_id = s.season_id¥nWHERE s.season_name = '2010'¥nGROUP BY g.game_id¥nHAVING COUNT(DISTINCT g.game_id) > 10;¥n```¥n¥n### Explanation of the Query:¥n¥n- **INNER JOIN**: We use `INNER JOIN` to ensure that we only consider records that have matching entries in the `games` and `seasons` tables.¥n- **WHERE Clause**: We filter the records to include only those from the 2010 season.¥n- **GROUP BY Clause**: We group the results by `game_id` to calculate the average goals per game.¥n- **HAVING Clause**: We ensure that only games with more than 10 distinct game IDs are considered.¥n¥nThis query should provide the desired result of the average number of goals scored per game in the 2010 season for games that have been played more than 10 times.
sql: SELECT AVG(pg.goals) AS avg_goals_per_game¥nFROM player_game_stats pg¥nINNER JOIN games g ON pg.game_id = g.game_id¥nINNER JOIN seasons s ON g.season_id = s.season_id¥nWHERE s.season_name = '2010'¥nGROUP BY g.game_id¥nHAVING COUNT(DISTINCT g.game_id) > 10;

question: Return the total games played, total goals, total assists, total penalty minutes, and total shots on goal for each team in every season, along with the names of the top three players for each team based on their individual goals and assists.
cot: To translate the natural language question into an executable SQL query, let's break down the problem step by step:¥n¥n1. **Understand the Question**: ¥n   - We need to return statistics for each team in every season, including total games played, total goals, total assists, total penalty minutes, and total shots on goal.¥n   - Additionally, for each team, we need to identify the top three players based on their combined goals and assists.¥n¥n2. **Identify Relevant Tables and Columns**:¥n   - `seasons`: `season_id`, `season_name`¥n   - `teams`: `team_id`, `team_name`¥n   - `games`: `game_id`, `season_id`, `home_team_id`, `away_team_id`¥n   - `player_game_stats`: `player_id`, `game_id`, `goals`, `assists`, `penalty_minutes`, `shots_on_goal`¥n   - `players`: `player_id`, `player_name`, `team_id`¥n¥n3. **Calculate Team Performance**:¥n   - We need to aggregate performance data for each team in each season. This involves grouping by `season_id` and `team_id`.¥n¥n4. **Identify Top Players**:¥n   - For each team, we need to rank players based on their total goals and assists. We can use the `ROW_NUMBER()` window function to achieve this.¥n¥n5. **Combine Results**:¥n   - We need to join the aggregated team performance data with the top players' data, ensuring that only the top three players are included for each team.¥n¥n6. **Construct the Query**:¥n   - Use Common Table Expressions (CTEs) to organize the query into logical steps.¥n¥nHere is the SQL query based on the above steps:¥n¥n```sql¥nWITH TeamPerformance AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        s.season_name,¥n        t.team_name,¥n        COUNT(DISTINCT g.game_id) AS total_games_played,¥n        SUM(pg.goals) AS total_goals,¥n        SUM(pg.assists) AS total_assists,¥n        SUM(pg.penalty_minutes) AS total_penalty_minutes,¥n        SUM(pg.shots_on_goal) AS total_shots_on_goal¥n    FROM seasons s¥n    JOIN games g ON s.season_id = g.season_id¥n    JOIN player_game_stats pg ON g.game_id = pg.game_id¥n    JOIN players p ON pg.player_id = p.player_id¥n    JOIN teams t ON p.team_id = t.team_id¥n    GROUP BY s.season_id, t.team_id¥n),¥nTopPlayers AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        s.season_name,¥n        t.team_name,¥n        p.player_name,¥n        SUM(pg.goals) AS total_goals,¥n        SUM(pg.assists) AS total_assists,¥n        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY s.season_id, t.team_id ORDER BY SUM(pg.goals + pg.assists) DESC) AS rank¥n    FROM seasons s¥n    JOIN games g ON s.season_id = g.season_id¥n    JOIN player_game_stats pg ON g.game_id = pg.game_id¥n    JOIN players p ON pg.player_id = p.player_id¥n    JOIN teams t ON p.team_id = t.team_id¥n    GROUP BY s.season_id, t.team_id, p.player_id¥n)¥nSELECT ¥n    tp.season_name,¥n    tp.team_name,¥n    tp.total_games_played,¥n    tp.total_goals,¥n    tp.total_assists,¥n    tp.total_penalty_minutes,¥n    tp.total_shots_on_goal,¥n    GROUP_CONCAT(tp2.player_name, ', ') AS top_performers¥nFROM TeamPerformance tp¥nLEFT JOIN TopPlayers tp2 ON tp.season_name = tp2.season_name AND tp.team_name = tp2.team_name AND tp2.rank <= 3¥nGROUP BY tp.season_name, tp.team_name¥nORDER BY tp.season_name, tp.team_name;¥n```¥n¥n### Explanation:¥n- **TeamPerformance CTE**: Aggregates team performance data for each season and team.¥n- **TopPlayers CTE**: Ranks players for each team in each season based on their total goals and assists.¥n- **Final SELECT**: Joins the team performance data with the top players' data, using `GROUP_CONCAT` to list the top three players for each team.
sql: WITH TeamPerformance AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        s.season_name,¥n        t.team_name,¥n        COUNT(DISTINCT g.game_id) AS total_games_played,¥n        SUM(pg.goals) AS total_goals,¥n        SUM(pg.assists) AS total_assists,¥n        SUM(pg.penalty_minutes) AS total_penalty_minutes,¥n        SUM(pg.shots_on_goal) AS total_shots_on_goal¥n    FROM seasons s¥n    JOIN games g ON s.season_id = g.season_id¥n    JOIN player_game_stats pg ON g.game_id = pg.game_id¥n    JOIN players p ON pg.player_id = p.player_id¥n    JOIN teams t ON p.team_id = t.team_id¥n    GROUP BY s.season_id, t.team_id¥n),¥nTopPlayers AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        s.season_name,¥n        t.team_name,¥n        p.player_name,¥n        SUM(pg.goals) AS total_goals,¥n        SUM(pg.assists) AS total_assists,¥n        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY s.season_id, t.team_id ORDER BY SUM(pg.goals + pg.assists) DESC) AS rank¥n    FROM seasons s¥n    JOIN games g ON s.season_id = g.season_id¥n    JOIN player_game_stats pg ON g.game_id = pg.game_id¥n    JOIN players p ON pg.player_id = p.player_id¥n    JOIN teams t ON p.team_id = t.team_id¥n    GROUP BY s.season_id, t.team_id, p.player_id¥n)¥nSELECT ¥n    tp.season_name,¥n    tp.team_name,¥n    tp.total_games_played,¥n    tp.total_goals,¥n    tp.total_assists,¥n    tp.total_penalty_minutes,¥n    tp.total_shots_on_goal,¥n    GROUP_CONCAT(tp2.player_name, ', ') AS top_performers¥nFROM TeamPerformance tp¥nLEFT JOIN TopPlayers tp2 ON tp.season_name = tp2.season_name AND tp.team_name = tp2.team_name AND tp2.rank <= 3¥nGROUP BY tp.season_name, tp.team_name¥nORDER BY tp.season_name, tp.team_name;

question: What are the names of the seasons, their average attendance, and the names of their respective champion teams for seasons that have more than 5 games, listed in chronological order?
cot: To translate the given natural language question into an executable SQLite query, let's break down the requirements and construct the query step-by-step.¥n¥n### Step-by-Step Solution¥n¥n1. **Identify the Required Tables and Columns**:¥n   - **seasons**: `season_name`, `champion_team_id`¥n   - **games**: `attendance`, `season_id`¥n   - **teams**: `team_name`, `team_id` (to get the champion team name)¥n¥n2. **Determine the Relationships**:¥n   - The `seasons` table is linked to the `games` table through `season_id`.¥n   - The `seasons` table is linked to the `teams` table through `champion_team_id`.¥n¥n3. **Formulate the Query**:¥n   - **Select Clause**: We need the `season_name` from `seasons`, the average `attendance` from `games`, and the `team_name` from `teams` for the champion team.¥n   - **Join Clause**: ¥n     - Join `seasons` with `games` on `season_id` to get the attendance for each season.¥n     - Join `seasons` with `teams` on `champion_team_id` to get the name of the champion team.¥n   - **Group By Clause**: Group by `season_name` and `team_name` to calculate the average attendance for each season and champion team.¥n   - **Having Clause**: Filter the results to include only seasons with more than 5 games.¥n   - **Order By Clause**: Order the results by `season_name` to list them in chronological order.¥n¥n4. **Construct the SQL Query**:¥n   - Start with the `SELECT` statement to specify the required columns.¥n   - Use `INNER JOIN` to combine `seasons` and `games` based on `season_id`.¥n   - Use `LEFT JOIN` to combine `seasons` and `teams` based on `champion_team_id`.¥n   - Apply the `GROUP BY` clause on `season_name` and `team_name`.¥n   - Use the `HAVING` clause to filter seasons with more than 5 games.¥n   - Finally, order the results by `season_name`.¥n¥n### Final SQL Query¥n¥n```sql¥nSELECT ¥n  s.season_name,¥n  AVG(g.attendance) AS average_attendance,¥n  t.team_name AS champion_team¥nFROM ¥n  seasons s¥n  INNER JOIN games g ON s.season_id = g.season_id¥n  LEFT JOIN teams t ON s.champion_team_id = t.team_id¥nGROUP BY ¥n  s.season_name, t.team_name¥nHAVING ¥n  COUNT(g.game_id) > 5  ¥nORDER BY ¥n  s.season_name;¥n```¥n¥nThis query meets all the requirements specified in the natural language question, ensuring that we get the names of the seasons, their average attendance, and the names of their respective champion teams for seasons with more than 5 games, listed in chronological order.
sql: SELECT ¥n  s.season_name,¥n  AVG(g.attendance) AS average_attendance,¥n  t.team_name AS champion_team¥nFROM ¥n  seasons s¥n  INNER JOIN games g ON s.season_id = g.season_id¥n  LEFT JOIN teams t ON s.champion_team_id = t.team_id¥nGROUP BY ¥n  s.season_name, t.team_name¥nHAVING ¥n  COUNT(g.game_id) > 5  ¥nORDER BY ¥n  s.season_name;

question: What is the highest total score a player has achieved in a single game, where the total score is the sum of goals and assists?
external_knowledge: Total score = goals + assists; The term 'total score' here refers to the sum of goals and assists a player achieves in a single game.
cot: To answer the natural language question, we need to find the highest total score a player has achieved in a single game, where the total score is the sum of goals and assists. Let's break down the problem step-by-step:¥n¥n1. **Understand the requirement**: We need to calculate the total score for each player in each game by summing their goals and assists. Then, we need to find the maximum total score among all these calculations.¥n¥n2. **Identify the relevant table**: The `player_game_stats` table contains the necessary information, including `player_id`, `game_id`, `goals`, and `assists`.¥n¥n3. **Calculate the total score for each player in each game**: We need to sum the `goals` and `assists` for each player in each game.¥n¥n4. **Find the highest total score**: We need to find the maximum value of the total scores calculated in the previous step.¥n¥nHere is the step-by-step SQL query:¥n¥n- **Step 1**: Select the `player_id` and `game_id` from the `player_game_stats` table.¥n- **Step 2**: Calculate the total score by adding `goals` and `assists`.¥n- **Step 3**: Group the results by `player_id` and `game_id` to get the total score for each player in each game.¥n- **Step 4**: Use a subquery to find the maximum total score from the results of the previous step.¥n¥nHere is the final SQL query:¥n¥n```sql¥nSELECT MAX(total_score) AS highest_total_score¥nFROM (¥n  SELECT player_id, game_id, (goals + assists) AS total_score¥n  FROM player_game_stats¥n);¥n```¥n¥n### Explanation:¥n¥n- **Subquery**: The inner query calculates the total score for each player in each game by summing up the `goals` and `assists` and selects `player_id`, `game_id`, and the calculated `total_score`.¥n- **Outer Query**: The outer query finds the maximum value of `total_score` from the results of the inner query, which gives us the highest total score a player has achieved in a single game.¥n¥n### Simplification and Validation:¥n¥n- The reference solution incorrectly groups by `player_id` and `game_id`, which is unnecessary for finding the highest total score. This can lead to more complex and slower queries.¥n- Our solution directly calculates the total score and finds the maximum, which is more efficient and aligns with the question's requirement.
sql: SELECT MAX(total_score) AS highest_total_score¥nFROM (¥n  SELECT player_id, game_id, (goals + assists) AS total_score¥n  FROM player_game_stats¥n);

question: What are the names of the top scorers, the total goals scored by them, the weather conditions, and the average attendance for the first two seasons, ordered by season and total goals scored?
external_knowledge: First two seasons refer to seasons with season_id = 0 and season_id = 1; seasons are typically ordered chronologically; top scorers refer to players with the highest total goals scored in a season.
cot: To translate the natural language question into an executable SQLite query, we need to break down the requirements and construct the query step-by-step.¥n¥n### Step-by-Step Solution¥n¥n1. **Identify the Tables and Columns Needed**:¥n   - **seasons**: `season_id`, `season_name`¥n   - **games**: `game_id`, `season_id`, `weather`, `attendance`¥n   - **player_game_stats**: `player_id`, `game_id`, `goals`¥n   - **players**: `player_id`, `player_name`¥n¥n2. **Filter the Seasons**:¥n   - We need data only for the first two seasons, which have `season_id` values of 0 and 1.¥n¥n3. **Calculate Total Goals for Each Player in Each Season**:¥n   - We need to sum up the goals scored by each player in each season.¥n¥n4. **Determine the Top Scorers**:¥n   - For each season, find the player with the highest total goals.¥n¥n5. **Include Weather and Average Attendance**:¥n   - For each top scorer, include the weather conditions and average attendance for the games in their season.¥n¥n6. **Order the Results**:¥n   - Order by season and total goals scored.¥n¥n### Construct the SQL Query¥n¥n1. **Subquery to Get Season Games**:¥n   - Join `seasons`, `games`, and `player_game_stats` to get the necessary data for the first two seasons.¥n¥n2. **Subquery to Get Top Scorers**:¥n   - Group by `season_id` and `player_id` to calculate total goals.¥n   - Order by total goals in descending order and limit to the top scorer per season.¥n¥n3. **Subquery to Get Weather Impact**:¥n   - Group by `season_id` and `weather` to calculate average attendance.¥n¥n4. **Final Query to Combine Results**:¥n   - Join the top scorers with the weather impact and select the required columns.¥n   - Order the final results by season and total goals.¥n¥nHere is the final SQL query:¥n¥n```sql¥nWITH SeasonGames AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        s.season_id, ¥n        s.season_name,¥n        g.game_id,¥n        g.weather,¥n        g.attendance,¥n        pgs.player_id,¥n        pgs.goals¥n    FROM ¥n        seasons s¥n    INNER JOIN ¥n        games g ON s.season_id = g.season_id¥n    INNER JOIN ¥n        player_game_stats pgs ON g.game_id = pgs.game_id¥n    WHERE ¥n        s.season_id IN (0, 1)¥n),¥nTopScorers AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        sg.season_id,¥n        sg.season_name,¥n        p.player_id,¥n        p.player_name,¥n        SUM(sg.goals) AS total_goals¥n    FROM ¥n        SeasonGames sg¥n    INNER JOIN ¥n        players p ON sg.player_id = p.player_id¥n    GROUP BY ¥n        sg.season_id, p.player_id¥n    ORDER BY ¥n        sg.season_id, total_goals DESC¥n),¥nWeatherImpact AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        sg.season_id,¥n        sg.season_name,¥n        sg.weather,¥n        AVG(sg.attendance) AS avg_attendance¥n    FROM ¥n        SeasonGames sg¥n    GROUP BY ¥n        sg.season_id, sg.weather¥n),¥nSeasonStats AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        ts.season_id,¥n        ts.season_name,¥n        ts.player_name,¥n        ts.total_goals,¥n        wi.weather,¥n        wi.avg_attendance¥n    FROM ¥n        TopScorers ts¥n    LEFT JOIN ¥n        WeatherImpact wi ON ts.season_id = wi.season_id¥n)¥nSELECT ¥n    ss.season_name,¥n    ss.player_name,¥n    ss.total_goals,¥n    ss.weather,¥n    ss.avg_attendance¥nFROM ¥n    SeasonStats ss¥nORDER BY ¥n    ss.season_name, ss.total_goals DESC;¥n```¥n¥n### Explanation¥n¥n- **SeasonGames CTE**: Joins `seasons`, `games`, and `player_game_stats` to get the data for the first two seasons.¥n- **TopScorers CTE**: Groups by `season_id` and `player_id` to calculate total goals and orders to get the top scorer per season.¥n- **WeatherImpact CTE**: Groups by `season_id` and `weather` to calculate average attendance.¥n- **SeasonStats CTE**: Joins `TopScorers` with `WeatherImpact` to combine the results.¥n- **Final SELECT**: Selects the required columns and orders the results by season and total goals.
sql: WITH SeasonGames AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        s.season_id, ¥n        s.season_name,¥n        g.game_id,¥n        g.weather,¥n        g.attendance,¥n        pgs.player_id,¥n        pgs.goals¥n    FROM ¥n        seasons s¥n    INNER JOIN ¥n        games g ON s.season_id = g.season_id¥n    INNER JOIN ¥n        player_game_stats pgs ON g.game_id = pgs.game_id¥n    WHERE ¥n        s.season_id IN (0, 1)¥n),¥nTopScorers AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        sg.season_id,¥n        sg.season_name,¥n        p.player_id,¥n        p.player_name,¥n        SUM(sg.goals) AS total_goals¥n    FROM ¥n        SeasonGames sg¥n    INNER JOIN ¥n        players p ON sg.player_id = p.player_id¥n    GROUP BY ¥n        sg.season_id, p.player_id¥n    ORDER BY ¥n        sg.season_id, total_goals DESC¥n),¥nWeatherImpact AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        sg.season_id,¥n        sg.season_name,¥n        sg.weather,¥n        AVG(sg.attendance) AS avg_attendance¥n    FROM ¥n        SeasonGames sg¥n    GROUP BY ¥n        sg.season_id, sg.weather¥n),¥nSeasonStats AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        ts.season_id,¥n        ts.season_name,¥n        ts.player_name,¥n        ts.total_goals,¥n        wi.weather,¥n        wi.avg_attendance¥n    FROM ¥n        TopScorers ts¥n    LEFT JOIN ¥n        WeatherImpact wi ON ts.season_id = wi.season_id¥n)¥nSELECT ¥n    ss.season_name,¥n    ss.player_name,¥n    ss.total_goals,¥n    ss.weather,¥n    ss.avg_attendance¥nFROM ¥n    SeasonStats ss¥nORDER BY ¥n    ss.season_name, ss.total_goals DESC;

question: Could you please gather all the game statistics for player ID 1, including the player's ID, game ID, date, location, weather, total goals, total assists, total penalty minutes, total plus-minus rating, total shots on goal, total faceoff wins and losses, total hits, total blocked shots, total giveaways, total takeaways, and total points, and rank these games by the total points scored in descending order?
cot: Let's break down the process of constructing the SQL query that addresses the given natural language question.¥n¥nThe question asks for all game statistics for a specific player (ID 1), including various metrics like goals, assists, penalty minutes, and more. It also requests that these statistics be aggregated per game and ranked by the total points scored in descending order.¥n¥n### Step-by-Step Solution:¥n¥n1. **Identify the Relevant Tables**: The required data is spread across two tables: `player_game_stats` for individual game statistics per player and `games` for additional game details like date, location, and weather.¥n¥n2. **Join the Tables**: Since we need game details along with player statistics, we must join the `player_game_stats` table with the `games` table on the `game_id` field.¥n¥n3. **Filter Data**: The query should be filtered to only include statistics for player ID 1. This is done using a `WHERE` clause.¥n¥n4. **Aggregate Data**: We need to aggregate game statistics per game. This involves summing up goals, assists, penalty minutes, etc., since a player can play multiple games.¥n¥n5. **Rank the Games**: After aggregating the data, we rank the games based on the total points scored by the player in each game. Total points are calculated as the sum of goals and assists.¥n¥n6. **Select the Required Columns**: The final query should select the required columns from the aggregated data, including the rank of each game.¥n¥n### Constructing the SQL Query:¥n¥n- **Starting with the `SELECT` clause**: We begin by selecting the columns we need. These include player ID, game ID, game date, location, weather, and all the aggregated statistics as well as the total points.¥n¥n- **Joining Tables**: We join `player_game_stats` (aliased as `pgs`) with `games` (aliased as `g`) on `game_id`.¥n¥n- **Filtering Data**: We apply a `WHERE` clause to filter the results to only include statistics for player ID 1.¥n¥n- **Aggregating Data**: We use the `SUM()` function to aggregate each required statistic across games.¥n¥n- **Ranking Data**: We use the `RANK()` window function to rank the games by total points in descending order.¥n¥n- **Finalizing the Query**: We wrap the entire query in a Common Table Expression (CTE) for clarity and then select from this CTE to finalize the output.¥n¥n```sql¥nWITH PlayerGameStatsCTE AS (¥n  SELECT¥n    pgs.player_id,¥n    pgs.game_id,¥n    g.date,¥n    g.location,¥n    g.weather,¥n    SUM(pgs.goals) AS total_goals,¥n    SUM(pgs.assists) AS total_assists,¥n    SUM(pgs.penalty_minutes) AS total_penalty_minutes,¥n    SUM(pgs.plus_minus) AS total_plus_minus,¥n    SUM(pgs.shots_on_goal) AS total_shots_on_goal,¥n    SUM(pgs.faceoff_wins) AS total_faceoff_wins,¥n    SUM(pgs.faceoff_losses) AS total_faceoff_losses,¥n    SUM(pgs.hits) AS total_hits,¥n    SUM(pgs.blocked_shots) AS total_blocked_shots,¥n    SUM(pgs.giveaways) AS total_giveaways,¥n    SUM(pgs.takeaways) AS total_takeaways,¥n    SUM(pgs.goals + pgs.assists) AS total_points¥n  FROM¥n    player_game_stats pgs¥n  INNER JOIN¥n    games g ON pgs.game_id = g.game_id¥n  WHERE¥n    pgs.player_id = 1  ¥n  GROUP BY¥n    pgs.player_id, pgs.game_id, g.date, g.location, g.weather¥n)¥nSELECT¥n  pgs.player_id,¥n  pgs.game_id,¥n  pgs.date,¥n  pgs.location,¥n  pgs.weather,¥n  pgs.total_goals,¥n  pgs.total_assists,¥n  pgs.total_penalty_minutes,¥n  pgs.total_plus_minus,¥n  pgs.total_shots_on_goal,¥n  pgs.total_faceoff_wins,¥n  pgs.total_faceoff_losses,¥n  pgs.total_hits,¥n  pgs.total_blocked_shots,¥n  pgs.total_giveaways,¥n  pgs.total_takeaways,¥n  pgs.total_points,¥n  RANK() OVER (ORDER BY pgs.total_points DESC) AS game_rank¥nFROM¥n  PlayerGameStatsCTE pgs;¥n```¥n¥nThis query provides the detailed game statistics for player ID 1 and ranks these games by total points scored in descending order.
sql: WITH PlayerGameStatsCTE AS (¥n  SELECT¥n    pgs.player_id,¥n    pgs.game_id,¥n    g.date,¥n    g.location,¥n    g.weather,¥n    SUM(pgs.goals) AS total_goals,¥n    SUM(pgs.assists) AS total_assists,¥n    SUM(pgs.penalty_minutes) AS total_penalty_minutes,¥n    SUM(pgs.plus_minus) AS total_plus_minus,¥n    SUM(pgs.shots_on_goal) AS total_shots_on_goal,¥n    SUM(pgs.faceoff_wins) AS total_faceoff_wins,¥n    SUM(pgs.faceoff_losses) AS total_faceoff_losses,¥n    SUM(pgs.hits) AS total_hits,¥n    SUM(pgs.blocked_shots) AS total_blocked_shots,¥n    SUM(pgs.giveaways) AS total_giveaways,¥n    SUM(pgs.takeaways) AS total_takeaways,¥n    SUM(pgs.goals + pgs.assists) AS total_points¥n  FROM¥n    player_game_stats pgs¥n  INNER JOIN¥n    games g ON pgs.game_id = g.game_id¥n  WHERE¥n    pgs.player_id = 1  ¥n  GROUP BY¥n    pgs.player_id, pgs.game_id, g.date, g.location, g.weather¥n)¥nSELECT¥n  pgs.player_id,¥n  pgs.game_id,¥n  pgs.date,¥n  pgs.location,¥n  pgs.weather,¥n  pgs.total_goals,¥n  pgs.total_assists,¥n  pgs.total_penalty_minutes,¥n  pgs.total_plus_minus,¥n  pgs.total_shots_on_goal,¥n  pgs.total_faceoff_wins,¥n  pgs.total_faceoff_losses,¥n  pgs.total_hits,¥n  pgs.total_blocked_shots,¥n  pgs.total_giveaways,¥n  pgs.total_takeaways,¥n  pgs.total_points,¥n  RANK() OVER (ORDER BY pgs.total_points DESC) AS game_rank¥nFROM¥n  PlayerGameStatsCTE pgs;

question: Which teams have an average goal score of more than half a goal per game, and what are their average goal scores?
external_knowledge: ; A goal score of more than half a goal per game indicates that a team, on average, scores at least one goal every two games, which can be considered as moderately scoring teams in a league or season.
cot: To solve the given natural language question, I will translate it into an executable SQLite query. I'll go through the process step-by-step and explain my reasoning for each part of the query.¥n¥n### Step-by-Step Solution¥n¥n**1. Understand the Question:**¥n   - The question asks for teams that have an average goal score of more than half a goal per game.¥n   - We need to calculate the average goals scored per game by each team and filter teams with an average greater than 0.5.¥n¥n**2. Identify Relevant Tables:**¥n   - **teams**: Contains team information (team_name).¥n   - **players**: Contains player information (player_id, team_id).¥n   - **player_game_stats**: Contains game statistics for each player (goals).¥n¥n**3. Formulate the Query:**¥n   - **SELECT Clause**: We need the team name and the calculated average goals.¥n     - `t.team_name`: Team name.¥n     - `AVG(pgs.goals) AS average_goals`: Average goals scored by the team.¥n¥n   - **FROM Clause**: Start with the teams table.¥n     - `FROM teams t`¥n¥n   - **JOIN Clauses**: Link players and their stats to the teams.¥n     - `JOIN players p ON p.team_id = t.team_id`: Link players to their respective teams.¥n     - `JOIN player_game_stats pgs ON p.player_id = pgs.player_id`: Link player stats to the players.¥n¥n   - **GROUP BY Clause**: Group the results by team.¥n     - `GROUP BY t.team_name`¥n¥n   - **HAVING Clause**: Filter teams with an average goal score greater than 0.5.¥n     - `HAVING AVG(pgs.goals) > 0.5`¥n¥n   - **ORDER BY Clause**: Order the results by average goals in descending order.¥n     - `ORDER BY average_goals DESC`¥n¥n**4. Final Query:**¥n   - Combine all parts to form the complete query.¥n¥n### Final SQL Query¥n¥n```sql¥nSELECT t.team_name, AVG(pgs.goals) AS average_goals¥nFROM teams t¥nJOIN players p ON p.team_id = t.team_id¥nJOIN player_game_stats pgs ON p.player_id = pgs.player_id¥nGROUP BY t.team_name¥nHAVING AVG(pgs.goals) > 0.5¥nORDER BY average_goals DESC;¥n```¥n¥n### Explanation of the Query¥n¥n- **Tables and Joins**: The query joins the `teams`, `players`, and `player_game_stats` tables to aggregate goal data by teams.¥n- **Aggregation**: The `AVG(pgs.goals)` function calculates the average goals scored per game by each team.¥n- **Filtering**: The `HAVING AVG(pgs.goals) > 0.5` clause filters out teams with an average goal score less than or equal to 0.5.¥n- **Ordering**: The `ORDER BY average_goals DESC` clause sorts the results in descending order of average goals.¥n¥nThis query ensures that only teams meeting the criteria are returned, and their average goals per game are displayed.
sql: SELECT t.team_name, AVG(pgs.goals) AS average_goals¥nFROM teams t¥nJOIN players p ON p.team_id = t.team_id¥nJOIN player_game_stats pgs ON p.player_id = pgs.player_id¥nGROUP BY t.team_name¥nHAVING AVG(pgs.goals) > 0.5¥nORDER BY average_goals DESC;

question: Which player had the highest points above the average points per game in their respective season, and what was their total points, games played, and average points per game for that season?
cot: Let's break down the natural language question and translate it into an executable SQLite query.¥n¥n### Step-by-Step Solution:¥n¥n1. **Understand the Question**: The question asks for the player who scored the highest points above the average points per game in their respective season. Additionally, it needs to provide the player's total points, games played, and average points per game for that season.¥n¥n2. **Identify Relevant Tables and Columns**:¥n   - **players**: To get player details.¥n   - **player_game_stats**: To get game-specific statistics like goals and assists.¥n   - **games**: To get game details like the season ID.¥n   - **seasons**: To get season details.¥n¥n3. **Calculate Total Points and Games Played**:¥n   - For each player-season combination, calculate the total points (sum of goals and assists).¥n   - Count the number of games played by each player in each season.¥n¥n4. **Calculate Average Points per Game**:¥n   - For each season, calculate the average points per game by dividing the total points by the number of games played for all players in that season.¥n¥n5. **Calculate Points Above Average**:¥n   - For each player-season combination, calculate the points above average by subtracting the season's average points per game from the player's average points per game.¥n¥n6. **Identify the Player with the Highest Points Above Average**:¥n   - Order the results by the season name and points above average in descending order.¥n   - Limit the result to the top record.¥n¥n### SQL Query:¥n¥n```sql¥nWITH PlayerSeasonStats AS (¥n    SELECT¥n        p.player_id,¥n        s.season_id,¥n        s.season_name,¥n        SUM(pgs.goals + pgs.assists) AS total_points,¥n        COUNT(g.game_id) AS games_played¥n    FROM¥n        players p¥n        INNER JOIN player_game_stats pgs ON p.player_id = pgs.player_id¥n        INNER JOIN games g ON pgs.game_id = g.game_id¥n        INNER JOIN seasons s ON g.season_id = s.season_id¥n    GROUP BY¥n        p.player_id,¥n        s.season_id,¥n        s.season_name¥n),¥nLeagueAverage AS (¥n    SELECT¥n        s.season_id,¥n        s.season_name,¥n        AVG(pss.total_points * 1.0 / pss.games_played) AS avg_points_per_game¥n    FROM¥n        seasons s¥n        INNER JOIN PlayerSeasonStats pss ON s.season_id = pss.season_id¥n    GROUP BY¥n        s.season_id,¥n        s.season_name¥n),¥nPlayerStatsWithAverages AS (¥n    SELECT¥n        pss.player_id,¥n        pss.season_id,¥n        pss.season_name,¥n        pss.total_points,¥n        pss.games_played,¥n        la.avg_points_per_game,¥n        (pss.total_points * 1.0 / pss.games_played) - la.avg_points_per_game AS points_above_average¥n    FROM¥n        PlayerSeasonStats pss¥n        INNER JOIN LeagueAverage la ON pss.season_id = la.season_id¥n)¥nSELECT¥n    psa.player_id,¥n    psa.season_id,¥n    psa.season_name,¥n    psa.total_points,¥n    psa.games_played,¥n    psa.avg_points_per_game,¥n    psa.points_above_average¥nFROM¥n    PlayerStatsWithAverages psa¥nORDER BY¥n    psa.season_name,¥n    psa.points_above_average DESC¥nLIMIT 1;¥n```¥n¥n### Explanation:¥n¥n- **PlayerSeasonStats**: Calculates the total points and games played for each player in each season.¥n- **LeagueAverage**: Calculates the average points per game for each season.¥n- **PlayerStatsWithAverages**: Combines the player-season statistics with the league average and calculates the points above average.¥n- **Final SELECT**: Orders the results by season name and points above average in descending order, then limits to the top record.¥n¥nThis query ensures that we get the player with the highest points above average for their respective season, along with their total points, games played, and average points per game.
sql: WITH PlayerSeasonStats AS (¥n    SELECT¥n        p.player_id,¥n        s.season_id,¥n        s.season_name,¥n        SUM(pgs.goals + pgs.assists) AS total_points,¥n        COUNT(g.game_id) AS games_played¥n    FROM¥n        players p¥n        INNER JOIN player_game_stats pgs ON p.player_id = pgs.player_id¥n        INNER JOIN games g ON pgs.game_id = g.game_id¥n        INNER JOIN seasons s ON g.season_id = s.season_id¥n    GROUP BY¥n        p.player_id,¥n        s.season_id,¥n        s.season_name¥n),¥nLeagueAverage AS (¥n    SELECT¥n        s.season_id,¥n        s.season_name,¥n        AVG(pss.total_points * 1.0 / pss.games_played) AS avg_points_per_game¥n    FROM¥n        seasons s¥n        INNER JOIN PlayerSeasonStats pss ON s.season_id = pss.season_id¥n    GROUP BY¥n        s.season_id,¥n        s.season_name¥n),¥nPlayerStatsWithAverages AS (¥n    SELECT¥n        pss.player_id,¥n        pss.season_id,¥n        pss.season_name,¥n        pss.total_points,¥n        pss.games_played,¥n        la.avg_points_per_game,¥n        (pss.total_points * 1.0 / pss.games_played) - la.avg_points_per_game AS points_above_average¥n    FROM¥n        PlayerSeasonStats pss¥n        INNER JOIN LeagueAverage la ON pss.season_id = la.season_id¥n)¥nSELECT¥n    psa.player_id,¥n    psa.season_id,¥n    psa.season_name,¥n    psa.total_points,¥n    psa.games_played,¥n    psa.avg_points_per_game,¥n    psa.points_above_average¥nFROM¥n    PlayerStatsWithAverages psa¥nORDER BY¥n    psa.season_name,¥n    psa.points_above_average DESC¥nLIMIT 1;

question: What are the team names and their average goals scored, for teams that have played at least one game?
cot: To translate the given natural language question into an executable SQLite query, let's break down the process step-by-step:¥n¥n1. **Identify the Required Information**:¥n   - The question asks for team names and their average goals scored.¥n   - We need to ensure that we only consider teams that have played at least one game.¥n¥n2. **Determine the Relevant Tables**:¥n   - **teams**: To get the team names.¥n   - **players**: To link players to their respective teams.¥n   - **player_game_stats**: To get the goals scored by each player in each game.¥n¥n3. **Formulate the SQL Query**:¥n   - **SELECT Clause**: We need to select the team name and calculate the average goals scored.¥n   - **FROM Clause**: Start with the `teams` table since we need team information.¥n   - **JOIN Clauses**: ¥n     - Join `players` on `team_id` to link each player to their team.¥n     - Join `player_game_stats` on `player_id` to get the game statistics for each player.¥n   - **GROUP BY Clause**: Group the results by team name to calculate the average goals for each team.¥n   - **HAVING Clause**: Ensure that only teams with at least one game are included by checking that the count of player-game statistics is greater than zero.¥n¥n4. **Construct the Query**:¥n   - Start with the `SELECT` statement to choose the team name and the average goals.¥n   - Use `JOINs` to combine the necessary tables based on the relationships.¥n   - Apply the `GROUP BY` to aggregate the data by team.¥n   - Include the `HAVING` clause to filter out teams with no game statistics.¥n¥n```sql¥nSELECT ¥n  t.team_name,¥n  AVG(pgs.goals) AS average_goals¥nFROM ¥n  teams t¥nJOIN ¥n  players p ON t.team_id = p.team_id¥nJOIN ¥n  player_game_stats pgs ON p.player_id = pgs.player_id¥nGROUP BY ¥n  t.team_name¥nHAVING ¥n  COUNT(pgs.player_game_stat_id) > 0;¥n```¥n¥nThis query will provide the team names along with their average goals scored for teams that have played at least one game.
sql: SELECT ¥n  t.team_name,¥n  AVG(pgs.goals) AS average_goals¥nFROM ¥n  teams t¥nJOIN ¥n  players p ON t.team_id = p.team_id¥nJOIN ¥n  player_game_stats pgs ON p.player_id = pgs.player_id¥nGROUP BY ¥n  t.team_name¥nHAVING ¥n  COUNT(pgs.player_game_stat_id) > 0;

question: Rank players by their average points per season and total games played, return their names and corresponding ranks.
cot: To solve the problem of ranking players by their average points per season and total games played, we need to derive the necessary data and compute the averages and ranks accordingly. Let's break down the steps to create the SQL query.¥n¥n### Step-by-Step Solution¥n¥n1. **Identify Relevant Tables and Columns**:¥n   - We need data from `players` (to get the player names) and `player_game_stats` (to get the game statistics including goals and assists).¥n   ¥n2. **Calculate Total Points**:¥n   - For each player, calculate the total points by summing up their goals and assists.¥n¥n3. **Count Total Games Played**:¥n   - Count the total number of games each player has played.¥n¥n4. **Calculate Average Points per Season**:¥n   - Calculate the average points per season for each player by dividing the total points by the number of seasons they have played.¥n¥n5. **Rank Players**:¥n   - Rank the players based on their average points per season and total games played.¥n¥n6. **Final Select**:¥n   - Select the player names and their corresponding ranks.¥n¥n### SQL Query¥n¥nBased on the above steps, here is the SQL query:¥n¥n```sql¥nWITH PlayerStats AS (¥n    SELECT¥n        pgs.player_id,¥n        SUM(pgs.goals + pgs.assists) AS total_points,¥n        COUNT(DISTINCT g.season_id) AS seasons_played,¥n        COUNT(DISTINCT pgs.game_id) AS games_played¥n    FROM player_game_stats pgs¥n    JOIN games g ON pgs.game_id = g.game_id¥n    GROUP BY pgs.player_id¥n),¥nPlayerPerformance AS (¥n    SELECT¥n        ps.player_id,¥n        ps.total_points,¥n        ps.seasons_played,¥n        ps.games_played,¥n        AVG(ps.total_points / ps.seasons_played) AS avg_points_per_season,¥n        RANK() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(ps.total_points / ps.seasons_played) DESC, ps.games_played DESC) AS rank_by_performance¥n    FROM PlayerStats ps¥n)¥nSELECT¥n    p.player_name,¥n    pp.rank_by_performance¥nFROM PlayerPerformance pp¥nJOIN players p ON pp.player_id = p.player_id¥nORDER BY pp.rank_by_performance ASC;¥n```¥n¥n### Explanation¥n¥n1. **PlayerStats CTE**:¥n   - This Common Table Expression (CTE) calculates the total points, number of seasons played, and total games played for each player.¥n   - `SUM(pgs.goals + pgs.assists) AS total_points`: Sums up the goals and assists for each player.¥n   - `COUNT(DISTINCT g.season_id) AS seasons_played`: Counts the distinct seasons in which each player has played.¥n   - `COUNT(DISTINCT pgs.game_id) AS games_played`: Counts the distinct games in which each player has played.¥n¥n2. **PlayerPerformance CTE**:¥n   - This CTE calculates the average points per season and ranks the players based on this average and total games played.¥n   - `AVG(ps.total_points / ps.seasons_played) AS avg_points_per_season`: Calculates the average points per season.¥n   - `RANK() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(ps.total_points / ps.seasons_played) DESC, ps.games_played DESC) AS rank_by_performance`: Ranks the players by their average points per season and total games played in descending order.¥n¥n3. **Final SELECT**:¥n   - Joins the `PlayerPerformance` CTE with the `players` table to get the player names.¥n   - Orders the results by the rank to present them in the correct order.
sql: WITH PlayerStats AS (¥n    SELECT¥n        pgs.player_id,¥n        SUM(pgs.goals + pgs.assists) AS total_points,¥n        COUNT(DISTINCT g.season_id) AS seasons_played,¥n        COUNT(DISTINCT pgs.game_id) AS games_played¥n    FROM player_game_stats pgs¥n    JOIN games g ON pgs.game_id = g.game_id¥n    GROUP BY pgs.player_id¥n),¥nPlayerPerformance AS (¥n    SELECT¥n        ps.player_id,¥n        ps.total_points,¥n        ps.seasons_played,¥n        ps.games_played,¥n        AVG(ps.total_points / ps.seasons_played) AS avg_points_per_season,¥n        RANK() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(ps.total_points / ps.seasons_played) DESC, ps.games_played DESC) AS rank_by_performance¥n    FROM PlayerStats ps¥n)¥nSELECT¥n    p.player_name,¥n    pp.rank_by_performance¥nFROM PlayerPerformance pp¥nJOIN players p ON pp.player_id = p.player_id¥nORDER BY pp.rank_by_performance ASC;

question: I want to know the names of the top-performing players in each season, along with the season name, total goals scored, total assists, total faceoff wins, and total games played, considering only players from the championship team and ranking them by their performance metrics in descending order.
cot: To address the natural language question, we need to identify the top-performing players from the championship team in each season. The performance metrics include total goals scored, total assists, total faceoff wins, and total games played. Here's how we can derive the SQL query step by step:¥n¥n### Step-by-Step Solution¥n¥n1. **Identify the Relevant Tables**:¥n   - **players**: Contains player details.¥n   - **player_game_stats**: Contains game-wise statistics for each player.¥n   - **games**: Contains game details, including the season ID.¥n   - **seasons**: Contains season details, including the champion team ID.¥n   - **teams**: Contains team details.¥n¥n2. **Join the Tables**:¥n   - We need to join these tables to get the required information:¥n     - `player_game_stats` with `players` to get player names.¥n     - `player_game_stats` with `games` to get the season ID.¥n     - `games` with `seasons` to get the champion team ID.¥n     - `players` with `teams` to filter players from the championship team.¥n¥n3. **Filter Players from the Championship Team**:¥n   - Use the condition `t.team_id = s.champion_team_id` to filter players who belong to the championship team of each season.¥n¥n4. **Aggregate Data**:¥n   - Aggregate the data to get the total goals, total assists, total faceoff wins, and total games played for each player in each season.¥n¥n5. **Rank Players**:¥n   - Rank the players within each season based on their performance metrics (total goals, total assists, total faceoff wins).¥n¥n6. **Select Top Performers**:¥n   - Select the top-performing player for each season (i.e., those with rank 1).¥n¥n7. **Order the Results**:¥n   - Order the results by season name in descending order and player name.¥n¥n### SQL Query¥n¥nHere's the derived SQL query based on the above steps:¥n¥n```sql¥nWITH PlayerSeasonPerformance AS (¥n    SELECT¥n        p.player_id,¥n        p.player_name,¥n        t.team_name,¥n        s.season_id,¥n        s.season_name,¥n        SUM(pgs.goals) AS total_goals,¥n        SUM(pgs.assists) AS total_assists,¥n        SUM(pgs.faceoff_wins) AS total_faceoff_wins,¥n        COUNT(pgs.game_id) AS total_games_played¥n    FROM¥n        players p¥n    JOIN¥n        player_game_stats pgs ON p.player_id = pgs.player_id¥n    JOIN¥n        games g ON pgs.game_id = g.game_id¥n    JOIN¥n        seasons s ON g.season_id = s.season_id¥n    JOIN¥n        teams t ON p.team_id = t.team_id¥n    WHERE¥n        t.team_id = s.champion_team_id¥n    GROUP BY¥n        p.player_id, p.player_name, t.team_name, s.season_id, s.season_name¥n),¥nTopPerformers AS (¥n    SELECT¥n        psp.player_id,¥n        psp.player_name,¥n        psp.team_name,¥n        psp.season_id,¥n        psp.season_name,¥n        psp.total_goals,¥n        psp.total_assists,¥n        psp.total_faceoff_wins,¥n        psp.total_games_played,¥n        RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY psp.season_id ORDER BY psp.total_goals DESC, psp.total_assists DESC, psp.total_faceoff_wins DESC) as rank¥n    FROM¥n        PlayerSeasonPerformance psp¥n)¥nSELECT¥n    tp.player_name,¥n    tp.season_name,¥n    tp.total_goals,¥n    tp.total_assists,¥n    tp.total_faceoff_wins,¥n    tp.total_games_played¥nFROM¥n    TopPerformers tp¥nWHERE¥n    tp.rank = 1¥nORDER BY¥n    tp.season_name DESC, tp.player_name;¥n```¥n¥nThis query should provide the names of the top-performing players from the championship team in each season, along with the required performance metrics.
sql: WITH PlayerSeasonPerformance AS (¥n    SELECT¥n        p.player_id,¥n        p.player_name,¥n        t.team_name,¥n        s.season_id,¥n        s.season_name,¥n        SUM(pgs.goals) AS total_goals,¥n        SUM(pgs.assists) AS total_assists,¥n        SUM(pgs.faceoff_wins) AS total_faceoff_wins,¥n        COUNT(pgs.game_id) AS total_games_played¥n    FROM¥n        players p¥n    JOIN¥n        player_game_stats pgs ON p.player_id = pgs.player_id¥n    JOIN¥n        games g ON pgs.game_id = g.game_id¥n    JOIN¥n        seasons s ON g.season_id = s.season_id¥n    JOIN¥n        teams t ON p.team_id = t.team_id¥n    WHERE¥n        t.team_id = s.champion_team_id¥n    GROUP BY¥n        p.player_id, p.player_name, t.team_name, s.season_id, s.season_name¥n),¥nTopPerformers AS (¥n    SELECT¥n        psp.player_id,¥n        psp.player_name,¥n        psp.team_name,¥n        psp.season_id,¥n        psp.season_name,¥n        psp.total_goals,¥n        psp.total_assists,¥n        psp.total_faceoff_wins,¥n        psp.total_games_played,¥n        RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY psp.season_id ORDER BY psp.total_goals DESC, psp.total_assists DESC, psp.total_faceoff_wins DESC) as rank¥n    FROM¥n        PlayerSeasonPerformance psp¥n)¥nSELECT¥n    tp.player_name,¥n    tp.season_name,¥n    tp.total_goals,¥n    tp.total_assists,¥n    tp.total_faceoff_wins,¥n    tp.total_games_played¥nFROM¥n    TopPerformers tp¥nWHERE¥n    tp.rank = 1¥nORDER BY¥n    tp.season_name DESC, tp.player_name;

question: Could you please identify the top-performing teams in each season, based on their average attendance and goal efficiency, and compare them to the actual champion teams of each season? I need to know the season names, champion team names, and the top-performing team names.
cot: To answer the natural language question, we need to identify the top-performing teams in each season based on their average attendance and goal efficiency, and compare them to the actual champion teams of each season. We need to extract the following information:¥n- Season names¥n- Champion team names¥n- Top-performing team names¥n¥n### Step-by-Step Solution¥n¥n1. **Identify Champion Teams**: ¥n   - Extract the season name and champion team name from the `seasons` table using the `champion_team_id` to join with the `teams` table.¥n¥n2. **Calculate Team Performance Metrics**:¥n   - Calculate the average attendance and goal efficiency for each team in each season.¥n   - Average attendance can be calculated directly from the `games` table.¥n   - Goal efficiency can be calculated as the ratio of average goals to average penalty minutes.¥n¥n3. **Determine Top-Performing Teams**:¥n   - Rank teams based on their average attendance and goal efficiency.¥n   - The team with the highest rank in each season will be considered the top-performing team.¥n¥n4. **Join and Select the Required Columns**:¥n   - Join the champion teams with the top-performing teams to get the required information.¥n¥n### SQL Query¥n¥n```sql¥nWITH ChampionTeams AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        s.season_id,¥n        s.season_name,¥n        t.team_name AS champion_team_name¥n    FROM seasons s¥n    JOIN teams t ON s.champion_team_id = t.team_id¥n),¥nTeamPerformance AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        g.season_id,¥n        t.team_id,¥n        t.team_name,¥n        AVG(g.attendance) AS avg_attendance,¥n        AVG(pgs.goals) AS avg_goals,¥n        AVG(pgs.penalty_minutes) AS avg_penalty_minutes,¥n        (AVG(pgs.goals) / AVG(pgs.penalty_minutes)) AS goal_efficiency¥n    FROM games g¥n    JOIN teams t ON g.home_team_id = t.team_id¥n    JOIN player_game_stats pgs ON g.game_id = pgs.game_id¥n    WHERE g.game_status = 'completed'¥n    GROUP BY g.season_id, t.team_id, t.team_name¥n),¥nTopPerformingTeams AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        tp.season_id,¥n        tp.team_name AS top_performing_team_name,¥n        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY tp.season_id ORDER BY tp.avg_attendance DESC, tp.goal_efficiency DESC) AS rank¥n    FROM TeamPerformance tp¥n)¥nSELECT ¥n    ct.season_name,¥n    ct.champion_team_name,¥n    tpt.top_performing_team_name¥nFROM ChampionTeams ct¥nJOIN TopPerformingTeams tpt ON ct.season_id = tpt.season_id AND tpt.rank = 1;¥n```¥n¥n### Explanation¥n¥n- **ChampionTeams CTE**: Extracts the season name and champion team name by joining the `seasons` and `teams` tables.¥n- **TeamPerformance CTE**: Calculates the average attendance and goal efficiency for each team in each season by joining the `games`, `teams`, and `player_game_stats` tables.¥n- **TopPerformingTeams CTE**: Ranks teams based on their average attendance and goal efficiency, with the top-ranked team in each season identified.¥n- **Final SELECT**: Joins the `ChampionTeams` and `TopPerformingTeams` CTEs to get the season name, champion team name, and top-performing team name for each season.
sql: WITH ChampionTeams AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        s.season_id,¥n        s.season_name,¥n        t.team_name AS champion_team_name¥n    FROM seasons s¥n    JOIN teams t ON s.champion_team_id = t.team_id¥n),¥nTeamPerformance AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        g.season_id,¥n        t.team_id,¥n        t.team_name,¥n        AVG(g.attendance) AS avg_attendance,¥n        AVG(pgs.goals) AS avg_goals,¥n        AVG(pgs.penalty_minutes) AS avg_penalty_minutes,¥n        (AVG(pgs.goals) / AVG(pgs.penalty_minutes)) AS goal_efficiency¥n    FROM games g¥n    JOIN teams t ON g.home_team_id = t.team_id¥n    JOIN player_game_stats pgs ON g.game_id = pgs.game_id¥n    WHERE g.game_status = 'completed'¥n    GROUP BY g.season_id, t.team_id, t.team_name¥n),¥nTopPerformingTeams AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        tp.season_id,¥n        tp.team_name AS top_performing_team_name,¥n        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY tp.season_id ORDER BY tp.avg_attendance DESC, tp.goal_efficiency DESC) AS rank¥n    FROM TeamPerformance tp¥n)¥nSELECT ¥n    ct.season_name,¥n    ct.champion_team_name,¥n    tpt.top_performing_team_name¥nFROM ChampionTeams ct¥nJOIN TopPerformingTeams tpt ON ct.season_id = tpt.season_id AND tpt.rank = 1;

question: What is the performance summary of the top player in each season, including their season, game details, player information, and team details?
cot: To solve the given natural language question, we need to identify the top player in each season based on their performance metrics (goals, assists, etc.) and then retrieve the necessary details about the season, game, player, and team. Here is the step-by-step breakdown of how we can achieve this:¥n¥n1. **Identify the Relevant Tables**: We need data from `seasons`, `games`, `players`, `player_game_stats`, and `teams`.¥n¥n2. **Join the Tables**: We will join these tables to get the required information. The key joins are:¥n   - `seasons` and `games` on `season_id`.¥n   - `games` and `player_game_stats` on `game_id`.¥n   - `player_game_stats` and `players` on `player_id`.¥n   - `players` and `teams` on `team_id`.¥n¥n3. **Calculate the Performance Metric**: We will calculate a performance metric by summing up the `goals` and `assists` for each player per season.¥n¥n4. **Rank Players**: We will rank players within each season based on their performance metric.¥n¥n5. **Select the Top Player**: We will select the top player (with rank 1) from each season.¥n¥n6. **Format the Output**: We will format the output as a JSON array for each season.¥n¥nHere is the SQL query to achieve the above steps:¥n¥n```sql¥nWITH PlayerSeasonStats AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        s.season_id,¥n        s.season_name,¥n        g.game_id,¥n        g.date,¥n        g.location,¥n        g.home_team_id,¥n        g.away_team_id,¥n        g.attendance,¥n        g.weather,¥n        pgs.player_id,¥n        p.player_name,¥n        p.team_id,¥n        p.position,¥n        p.number,¥n        p.birth_date,¥n        p.height,¥n        p.weight,¥n        p.nationality,¥n        p.draft_year,¥n        p.draft_round,¥n        p.draft_pick,¥n        SUM(pgs.goals) AS total_goals,¥n        SUM(pgs.assists) AS total_assists,¥n        SUM(pgs.penalty_minutes) AS total_penalty_minutes,¥n        SUM(pgs.plus_minus) AS total_plus_minus,¥n        SUM(pgs.time_on_ice) AS total_time_on_ice,¥n        SUM(pgs.faceoff_wins) AS total_faceoff_wins,¥n        SUM(pgs.faceoff_losses) AS total_faceoff_losses,¥n        SUM(pgs.hits) AS total_hits,¥n        SUM(pgs.blocked_shots) AS total_blocked_shots,¥n        SUM(pgs.giveaways) AS total_giveaways,¥n        SUM(pgs.takeaways) AS total_takeaways,¥n        SUM(pgs.shots_on_goal) AS total_shots_on_goal,¥n        SUM(pgs.power_play_goals) AS total_power_play_goals,¥n        SUM(pgs.power_play_assists) AS total_power_play_assists,¥n        SUM(pgs.short_handed_goals) AS total_short_handed_goals,¥n        SUM(pgs.short_handed_assists) AS total_short_handed_assists,¥n        t.team_name,¥n        t.team_abbreviation,¥n        t.city,¥n        t.arena,¥n        t.coach,¥n        t.captain,¥n        t.team_logo,¥n        t.team_color¥n    FROM seasons s¥n    INNER JOIN games g ON s.season_id = g.season_id¥n    INNER JOIN player_game_stats pgs ON g.game_id = pgs.game_id¥n    INNER JOIN players p ON pgs.player_id = p.player_id¥n    INNER JOIN teams t ON p.team_id = t.team_id¥n    GROUP BY s.season_id, s.season_name, g.game_id, g.date, g.location, g.home_team_id, g.away_team_id, g.attendance, g.weather, pgs.player_id, p.player_name, p.team_id, p.position, p.number, p.birth_date, p.height, p.weight, p.nationality, p.draft_year, p.draft_round, p.draft_pick, t.team_name, t.team_abbreviation, t.city, t.arena, t.coach, t.captain, t.team_logo, t.team_color¥n),¥nRankedPlayers AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        pss.season_id,¥n        pss.season_name,¥n        pss.game_id,¥n        pss.date,¥n        pss.location,¥n        pss.home_team_id,¥n        pss.away_team_id,¥n        pss.attendance,¥n        pss.weather,¥n        pss.player_id,¥n        pss.player_name,¥n        pss.team_id,¥n        pss.position,¥n        pss.number,¥n        pss.birth_date,¥n        pss.height,¥n        pss.weight,¥n        pss.nationality,¥n        pss.draft_year,¥n        pss.draft_round,¥n        pss.draft_pick,¥n        pss.total_goals,¥n        pss.total_assists,¥n        pss.total_penalty_minutes,¥n        pss.total_plus_minus,¥n        pss.total_time_on_ice,¥n        pss.total_faceoff_wins,¥n        pss.total_faceoff_losses,¥n        pss.total_hits,¥n        pss.total_blocked_shots,¥n        pss.total_giveaways,¥n        pss.total_takeaways,¥n        pss.total_shots_on_goal,¥n        pss.total_power_play_goals,¥n        pss.total_power_play_assists,¥n        pss.total_short_handed_goals,¥n        pss.total_short_handed_assists,¥n        pss.team_name,¥n        pss.team_abbreviation,¥n        pss.city,¥n        pss.arena,¥n        pss.coach,¥n        pss.captain,¥n        pss.team_logo,¥n        pss.team_color,¥n        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY pss.season_id ORDER BY pss.total_goals DESC, pss.total_assists DESC) AS rank¥n    FROM PlayerSeasonStats pss¥n)¥nSELECT ¥n    json_group_array(¥n        json_object(¥n            'season_id', rps.season_id,¥n            'season_name', rps.season_name,¥n            'game_id', rps.game_id,¥n            'date', rps.date,¥n            'location', rps.location,¥n            'home_team_id', rps.home_team_id,¥n            'away_team_id', rps.away_team_id,¥n            'attendance', rps.attendance,¥n            'weather', rps.weather,¥n            'player_id', rps.player_id,¥n            'player_name', rps.player_name,¥n            'position', rps.position,¥n            'number', rps.number,¥n            'birth_date', rps.birth_date,¥n            'height', rps.height,¥n            'weight', rps.weight,¥n            'nationality', rps.nationality,¥n            'draft_year', rps.draft_year,¥n            'draft_round', rps.draft_round,¥n            'draft_pick', rps.draft_pick,¥n            'total_goals', rps.total_goals,¥n            'total_assists', rps.total_assists,¥n            'total_penalty_minutes', rps.total_penalty_minutes,¥n            'total_plus_minus', rps.total_plus_minus,¥n            'total_time_on_ice', rps.total_time_on_ice,¥n            'total_faceoff_wins', rps.total_faceoff_wins,¥n            'total_faceoff_losses', rps.total_faceoff_losses,¥n            'total_hits', rps.total_hits,¥n            'total_blocked_shots', rps.total_blocked_shots,¥n            'total_giveaways', rps.total_giveaways,¥n            'total_takeaways', rps.total_takeaways,¥n            'total_shots_on_goal', rps.total_shots_on_goal,¥n            'total_power_play_goals', rps.total_power_play_goals,¥n            'total_power_play_assists', rps.total_power_play_assists,¥n            'total_short_handed_goals', rps.total_short_handed_goals,¥n            'total_short_handed_assists', rps.total_short_handed_assists,¥n            'team_name', rps.team_name,¥n            'team_abbreviation', rps.team_abbreviation,¥n            'city', rps.city,¥n            'arena', rps.arena,¥n            'coach', rps.coach,¥n            'captain', rps.captain,¥n            'team_logo', rps.team_logo,¥n            'team_color', rps.team_color,¥n            'rank', rps.rank¥n        )¥n    ) AS player_performance_summary¥nFROM RankedPlayers rps¥nWHERE rps.rank = 1¥nGROUP BY rps.season_id;¥n```¥n¥n### Explanation:¥n1. **PlayerSeasonStats CTE**: This common table expression (CTE) calculates the total performance metrics for each player in each season.¥n2. **RankedPlayers CTE**: This CTE ranks the players within each season based on their total goals and assists.¥n3. **Final SELECT**: This query selects the top player (rank 1) in each season and formats the result as a JSON array.¥n¥nThis query should provide the performance summary of the top player in each season, including the required details about the season, game, player, and team.
sql: WITH PlayerSeasonStats AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        s.season_id,¥n        s.season_name,¥n        g.game_id,¥n        g.date,¥n        g.location,¥n        g.home_team_id,¥n        g.away_team_id,¥n        g.attendance,¥n        g.weather,¥n        pgs.player_id,¥n        p.player_name,¥n        p.team_id,¥n        p.position,¥n        p.number,¥n        p.birth_date,¥n        p.height,¥n        p.weight,¥n        p.nationality,¥n        p.draft_year,¥n        p.draft_round,¥n        p.draft_pick,¥n        SUM(pgs.goals) AS total_goals,¥n        SUM(pgs.assists) AS total_assists,¥n        SUM(pgs.penalty_minutes) AS total_penalty_minutes,¥n        SUM(pgs.plus_minus) AS total_plus_minus,¥n        SUM(pgs.time_on_ice) AS total_time_on_ice,¥n        SUM(pgs.faceoff_wins) AS total_faceoff_wins,¥n        SUM(pgs.faceoff_losses) AS total_faceoff_losses,¥n        SUM(pgs.hits) AS total_hits,¥n        SUM(pgs.blocked_shots) AS total_blocked_shots,¥n        SUM(pgs.giveaways) AS total_giveaways,¥n        SUM(pgs.takeaways) AS total_takeaways,¥n        SUM(pgs.shots_on_goal) AS total_shots_on_goal,¥n        SUM(pgs.power_play_goals) AS total_power_play_goals,¥n        SUM(pgs.power_play_assists) AS total_power_play_assists,¥n        SUM(pgs.short_handed_goals) AS total_short_handed_goals,¥n        SUM(pgs.short_handed_assists) AS total_short_handed_assists,¥n        t.team_name,¥n        t.team_abbreviation,¥n        t.city,¥n        t.arena,¥n        t.coach,¥n        t.captain,¥n        t.team_logo,¥n        t.team_color¥n    FROM seasons s¥n    INNER JOIN games g ON s.season_id = g.season_id¥n    INNER JOIN player_game_stats pgs ON g.game_id = pgs.game_id¥n    INNER JOIN players p ON pgs.player_id = p.player_id¥n
