played by each player within a season.¥n   - Rank players based on total goals and assists within each season.¥n¥n5. **Calculate Overall Statistics:**¥n   - Sum up goals, assists, and penalty minutes for each player across all seasons.¥n¥n6. **Rank Players:**¥n   - Use the `RANK()` function to rank players based on total goals and assists within each season.¥n¥n7. **Order the Results:**¥n   - Order by season ID and player rank.¥n¥n### SQL Query:¥n¥n```sql¥nWITH PlayerSeasonStats AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        pgs.player_id,¥n        g.season_id,¥n        SUM(pgs.goals) AS total_goals,¥n        SUM(pgs.assists) AS total_assists,¥n        SUM(pgs.penalty_minutes) AS total_penalty_minutes,¥n        COUNT(g.game_id) AS games_played¥n    FROM ¥n        player_game_stats pgs¥n    JOIN ¥n        games g ON pgs.game_id = g.game_id¥n    GROUP BY ¥n        pgs.player_id, g.season_id¥n),¥nPlayerOverallStats AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        pgs.player_id,¥n        SUM(pgs.goals) AS overall_goals,¥n        SUM(pgs.assists) AS overall_assists,¥n        SUM(pgs.penalty_minutes) AS overall_penalty_minutes,¥n        COUNT(g.game_id) AS total_games_played¥n    FROM ¥n        player_game_stats pgs¥n    JOIN ¥n        games g ON pgs.game_id = g.game_id¥n    GROUP BY ¥n        pgs.player_id¥n),¥nRankedPlayerStats AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        pss.player_id,¥n        pss.season_id,¥n        s.season_name,¥n        pss.total_goals,¥n        pss.total_assists,¥n        pss.total_penalty_minutes,¥n        pss.games_played,¥n        RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY pss.season_id ORDER BY pss.total_goals DESC, pss.total_assists DESC) AS player_rank,¥n        pos.overall_goals,¥n        pos.overall_assists,¥n        pos.overall_penalty_minutes,¥n        pos.total_games_played,¥n        s.number_of_teams,¥n        t.team_name,¥n        t.team_abbreviation¥n    FROM ¥n        PlayerSeasonStats pss¥n    JOIN ¥n        PlayerOverallStats pos ON pss.player_id = pos.player_id¥n    JOIN ¥n        players p ON pss.player_id = p.player_id¥n    JOIN ¥n        teams t ON p.team_id = t.team_id¥n    JOIN ¥n        seasons s ON pss.season_id = s.season_id¥n)¥nSELECT ¥n    rps.player_id,¥n    rps.season_id,¥n    rps.season_name,¥n    rps.total_goals,¥n    rps.total_assists,¥n    rps.total_penalty_minutes,¥n    rps.games_played,¥n    rps.player_rank,¥n    rps.overall_goals,¥n    rps.overall_assists,¥n    rps.overall_penalty_minutes,¥n    rps.total_games_played,¥n    rps.number_of_teams,¥n    rps.team_name,¥n    rps.team_abbreviation¥nFROM ¥n    RankedPlayerStats rps¥nORDER BY ¥n    rps.season_id, rps.player_rank;¥n```¥n¥n### Explanation:¥n¥n- **PlayerSeasonStats**: Aggregates player stats for each season.¥n- **PlayerOverallStats**: Aggregates player stats across all seasons.¥n- **RankedPlayerStats**: Joins all necessary tables and ranks players within each season.¥n- **Final SELECT**: Retrieves the required columns and orders the results by season ID and player rank.¥n¥nThis query ensures that all required data is aggregated and ranked correctly, and it avoids unnecessary table and column selections, as well as excessive joins.
sql: WITH PlayerSeasonStats AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        pgs.player_id,¥n        g.season_id,¥n        SUM(pgs.goals) AS total_goals,¥n        SUM(pgs.assists) AS total_assists,¥n        SUM(pgs.penalty_minutes) AS total_penalty_minutes,¥n        COUNT(g.game_id) AS games_played¥n    FROM ¥n        player_game_stats pgs¥n    JOIN ¥n        games g ON pgs.game_id = g.game_id¥n    GROUP BY ¥n        pgs.player_id, g.season_id¥n),¥nPlayerOverallStats AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        pgs.player_id,¥n        SUM(pgs.goals) AS overall_goals,¥n        SUM(pgs.assists) AS overall_assists,¥n        SUM(pgs.penalty_minutes) AS overall_penalty_minutes,¥n        COUNT(g.game_id) AS total_games_played¥n    FROM ¥n        player_game_stats pgs¥n    JOIN ¥n        games g ON pgs.game_id = g.game_id¥n    GROUP BY ¥n        pgs.player_id¥n),¥nRankedPlayerStats AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        pss.player_id,¥n        pss.season_id,¥n        s.season_name,¥n        pss.total_goals,¥n        pss.total_assists,¥n        pss.total_penalty_minutes,¥n        pss.games_played,¥n        RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY pss.season_id ORDER BY pss.total_goals DESC, pss.total_assists DESC) AS player_rank,¥n        pos.overall_goals,¥n        pos.overall_assists,¥n        pos.overall_penalty_minutes,¥n        pos.total_games_played,¥n        s.number_of_teams,¥n        t.team_name,¥n        t.team_abbreviation¥n    FROM ¥n        PlayerSeasonStats pss¥n    JOIN ¥n        PlayerOverallStats pos ON pss.player_id = pos.player_id¥n    JOIN ¥n        players p ON pss.player_id = p.player_id¥n    JOIN ¥n        teams t ON p.team_id = t.team_id¥n    JOIN ¥n        seasons s ON pss.season_id = s.season_id¥n)¥nSELECT ¥n    rps.player_id,¥n    rps.season_id,¥n    rps.season_name,¥n    rps.total_goals,¥n    rps.total_assists,¥n    rps.total_penalty_minutes,¥n    rps.games_played,¥n    rps.player_rank,¥n    rps.overall_goals,¥n    rps.overall_assists,¥n    rps.overall_penalty_minutes,¥n    rps.total_games_played,¥n    rps.number_of_teams,¥n    rps.team_name,¥n    rps.team_abbreviation¥nFROM ¥n    RankedPlayerStats rps¥nORDER BY ¥n    rps.season_id, rps.player_rank;

question: Could you please gather all the game IDs for analysts where players have shown improvement in at least one performance metric compared to their previous game, and provide the corresponding season information and player performance data?
cot: To tackle the provided natural language question, let's break it down into a series of logical steps:¥n¥n### Step 1: Understand the Task¥nWe need to find game IDs where players have shown improvement in at least one performance metric compared to their previous game. Additionally, we need to include the season information and player performance data for these games.¥n¥n### Step 2: Identify Relevant Tables¥n1. **player_game_stats**: Contains performance metrics for each game a player played.¥n2. **games**: Contains game details, including the season ID.¥n3. **seasons**: Contains season details.¥n¥n### Step 3: Determine the Improvement Criteria¥nWe need to compare the current game's metrics to the previous game's metrics for each player. The improvement is determined by checking if any of the metrics have increased.¥n¥n### Step 4: Use Window Functions¥nWe will use the `LAG` window function to get the previous game's metrics for each player.¥n¥n### Step 5: Filter Games with Improvement¥nWe will filter the games where at least one metric has improved.¥n¥n### Step 6: Join Tables for Complete Data¥nWe will join the filtered games with the games and seasons tables to get the required season information.¥n¥n### Step 7: Ensure the Analyst Information is Included¥nAs the question specifies that analysts are involved, we need to ensure that the user roles are checked, but since the question does not ask for user details, we will not include them in the final output.¥n¥n### Step 8: Construct the SQL Query¥nLet's construct the SQL query step by step:¥n¥n1. **PlayerPerformance CTE**: Calculate the previous game's metrics for each player.¥n2. **FilteredPerformance CTE**: Filter out the games where at least one metric has improved.¥n3. **SeasonInfo CTE**: Get the season information for each game.¥n4. **FinalJoin CTE**: Join the filtered performance data with the season information.¥n5. **Select the required columns**: Finally, select the game IDs.¥n¥n```sql¥nWITH PlayerPerformance AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        player_id,¥n        game_id,¥n        goals,¥n        assists,¥n        penalty_minutes,¥n        plus_minus,¥n        time_on_ice,¥n        faceoff_wins,¥n        faceoff_losses,¥n        hits,¥n        blocked_shots,¥n        giveaways,¥n        takeaways,¥n        shots_on_goal,¥n        power_play_goals,¥n        power_play_assists,¥n        short_handed_goals,¥n        short_handed_assists,¥n        LAG(goals) OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY game_id) AS prev_goals,¥n        LAG(assists) OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY game_id) AS prev_assists,¥n        LAG(penalty_minutes) OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY game_id) AS prev_penalty_minutes,¥n        LAG(plus_minus) OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY game_id) AS prev_plus_minus,¥n        LAG(time_on_ice) OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY game_id) AS prev_time_on_ice,¥n        LAG(faceoff_wins) OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY game_id) AS prev_faceoff_wins,¥n        LAG(faceoff_losses) OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY game_id) AS prev_faceoff_losses,¥n        LAG(hits) OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY game_id) AS prev_hits,¥n        LAG(blocked_shots) OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY game_id) AS prev_blocked_shots,¥n        LAG(giveaways) OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY game_id) AS prev_giveaways,¥n        LAG(takeaways) OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY game_id) AS prev_takeaways,¥n        LAG(shots_on_goal) OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY game_id) AS prev_shots_on_goal,¥n        LAG(power_play_goals) OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY game_id) AS prev_power_play_goals,¥n        LAG(power_play_assists) OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY game_id) AS prev_power_play_assists,¥n        LAG(short_handed_goals) OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY game_id) AS prev_short_handed_goals,¥n        LAG(short_handed_assists) OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY game_id) AS prev_short_handed_assists¥n    FROM player_game_stats¥n),¥nFilteredPerformance AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        player_id,¥n        game_id,¥n        goals - prev_goals AS goal_improvement,¥n        assists - prev_assists AS assist_improvement,¥n        penalty_minutes - prev_penalty_minutes AS penalty_improvement,¥n        plus_minus - prev_plus_minus AS plus_minus_improvement,¥n        time_on_ice,¥n        faceoff_wins - prev_faceoff_wins AS faceoff_improvement,¥n        hits - prev_hits AS hit_improvement,¥n        blocked_shots - prev_blocked_shots AS blocked_shot_improvement,¥n        giveaways - prev_giveaways AS giveaway_improvement,¥n        takeaways - prev_takeaways AS takeaway_improvement,¥n        shots_on_goal - prev_shots_on_goal AS shot_improvement,¥n        power_play_goals - prev_power_play_goals AS power_play_goal_improvement,¥n        power_play_assists - prev_power_play_assists AS power_play_assist_improvement,¥n        short_handed_goals - prev_short_handed_goals AS short_handed_goal_improvement,¥n        short_handed_assists - prev_short_handed_assists AS short_handed_assist_improvement¥n    FROM PlayerPerformance¥n    WHERE (goals - prev_goals > 0 OR¥n           assists - prev_assists > 0 OR¥n           plus_minus - prev_plus_minus > 0 OR¥n           faceoff_wins - prev_faceoff_wins > 0 OR¥n           hits - prev_hits > 0 OR¥n           blocked_shots - prev_blocked_shots > 0 OR¥n           giveaways - prev_giveaways > 0 OR¥n           takeaways - prev_takeaways > 0 OR¥n           shots_on_goal - prev_shots_on_goal > 0 OR¥n           power_play_goals - prev_power_play_goals > 0 OR¥n           power_play_assists - prev_power_play_assists > 0 OR¥n           short_handed_goals - prev_short_handed_goals > 0 OR¥n           short_handed_assists - prev_short_handed_assists > 0)¥n),¥nSeasonInfo AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        g.game_id,¥n        s.season_name,¥n        s.number_of_teams¥n    FROM games g¥n    INNER JOIN seasons s ON g.season_id = s.season_id¥n),¥nFinalJoin AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        fp.game_id,¥n        s.season_name,¥n        s.number_of_teams,¥n        fp.goal_improvement,¥n        fp.assist_improvement,¥n        fp.penalty_improvement,¥n        fp.plus_minus_improvement,¥n        fp.time_on_ice,¥n        fp.faceoff_improvement,¥n        fp.hit_improvement,¥n        fp.blocked_shot_improvement,¥n        fp.giveaway_improvement,¥n        fp.takeaway_improvement,¥n        fp.shot_improvement,¥n        fp.power_play_goal_improvement,¥n        fp.power_play_assist_improvement,¥n        fp.short_handed_goal_improvement,¥n        fp.short_handed_assist_improvement¥n    FROM FilteredPerformance fp¥n    INNER JOIN SeasonInfo s ON fp.game_id = s.game_id¥n)¥nSELECT ¥n    fj.game_id,¥n    fj.season_name,¥n    fj.number_of_teams,¥n    fj.goal_improvement,¥n    fj.assist_improvement,¥n    fj.penalty_improvement,¥n    fj.plus_minus_improvement,¥n    fj.time_on_ice,¥n    fj.faceoff_improvement,¥n    fj.hit_improvement,¥n    fj.blocked_shot_improvement,¥n    fj.giveaway_improvement,¥n    fj.takeaway_improvement,¥n    fj.shot_improvement,¥n    fj.power_play_goal_improvement,¥n    fj.power_play_assist_improvement,¥n    fj.short_handed_goal_improvement,¥n    fj.short_handed_assist_improvement¥nFROM FinalJoin fj¥nORDER BY fj.game_id;¥n```¥n¥nThis query should provide the required game IDs along with the corresponding season information and player performance data for games where players have shown improvement in at least one performance metric compared to their previous game.
sql: WITH PlayerPerformance AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        player_id,¥n        game_id,¥n        goals,¥n        assists,¥n        penalty_minutes,¥n        plus_minus,¥n        time_on_ice,¥n        faceoff_wins,¥n        faceoff_losses,¥n        hits,¥n        blocked_shots,¥n        giveaways,¥n        takeaways,¥n        shots_on_goal,¥n        power_play_goals,¥n        power_play_assists,¥n        short_handed_goals,¥n        short_handed_assists,¥n        LAG(goals) OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY game_id) AS prev_goals,¥n        LAG(assists) OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY game_id) AS prev_assists,¥n        LAG(penalty_minutes) OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY game_id) AS prev_penalty_minutes,¥n        LAG(plus_minus) OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY game_id) AS prev_plus_minus,¥n        LAG(time_on_ice) OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY game_id) AS prev_time_on_ice,¥n        LAG(faceoff_wins) OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY game_id) AS prev_faceoff_wins,¥n        LAG(faceoff_losses) OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY game_id) AS prev_faceoff_losses,¥n        LAG(hits) OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY game_id) AS prev_hits,¥n        LAG(blocked_shots) OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY game_id) AS prev_blocked_shots,¥n        LAG(giveaways) OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY game_id) AS prev_giveaways,¥n        LAG(takeaways) OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY game_id) AS prev_takeaways,¥n        LAG(shots_on_goal) OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY game_id) AS prev_shots_on_goal,¥n        LAG(power_play_goals) OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY game_id) AS prev_power_play_goals,¥n        LAG(power_play_assists) OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY game_id) AS prev_power_play_assists,¥n        LAG(short_handed_goals) OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY game_id) AS prev_short_handed_goals,¥n        LAG(short_handed_assists) OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY game_id) AS prev_short_handed_assists¥n    FROM player_game_stats¥n),¥nFilteredPerformance AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        player_id,¥n        game_id,¥n        goals - prev_goals AS goal_improvement,¥n        assists - prev_assists AS assist_improvement,¥n        penalty_minutes - prev_penalty_minutes AS penalty_improvement,¥n        plus_minus - prev_plus_minus AS plus_minus_improvement,¥n        time_on_ice,¥n        faceoff_wins - prev_faceoff_wins AS faceoff_improvement,¥n        hits - prev_hits AS hit_improvement,¥n        blocked_shots - prev_blocked_shots AS blocked_shot_improvement,¥n        giveaways - prev_giveaways AS giveaway_improvement,¥n        takeaways - prev_takeaways AS takeaway_improvement,¥n        shots_on_goal - prev_shots_on_goal AS shot_improvement,¥n        power_play_goals - prev_power_play_goals AS power_play_goal_improvement,¥n        power_play_assists - prev_power_play_assists AS power_play_assist_improvement,¥n        short_handed_goals - prev_short_handed_goals AS short_handed_goal_improvement,¥n        short_handed_assists - prev_short_handed_assists AS short_handed_assist_improvement¥n    FROM PlayerPerformance¥n    WHERE (goals - prev_goals > 0 OR¥n           assists - prev_assists > 0 OR¥n           plus_minus - prev_plus_minus > 0 OR¥n           faceoff_wins - prev_faceoff_wins > 0 OR¥n           hits - prev_hits > 0 OR¥n           blocked_shots - prev_blocked_shots > 0 OR¥n           giveaways - prev_giveaways > 0 OR¥n           takeaways - prev_takeaways > 0 OR¥n           shots_on_goal - prev_shots_on_goal > 0 OR¥n           power_play_goals - prev_power_play_goals > 0 OR¥n           power_play_assists - prev_power_play_assists > 0 OR¥n           short_handed_goals - prev_short_handed_goals > 0 OR¥n           short_handed_assists - prev_short_handed_assists > 0)¥n),¥nSeasonInfo AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        g.game_id,¥n        s.season_name,¥n        s.number_of_teams¥n    FROM games g¥n    INNER JOIN seasons s ON g.season_id = s.season_id¥n),¥nFinalJoin AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        fp.game_id,¥n        s.season_name,¥n        s.number_of_teams,¥n        fp.goal_improvement,¥n        fp.assist_improvement,¥n        fp.penalty_improvement,¥n        fp.plus_minus_improvement,¥n        fp.time_on_ice,¥n        fp.faceoff_improvement,¥n        fp.hit_improvement,¥n        fp.blocked_shot_improvement,¥n        fp.giveaway_improvement,¥n        fp.takeaway_improvement,¥n        fp.shot_improvement,¥n        fp.power_play_goal_improvement,¥n        fp.power_play_assist_improvement,¥n        fp.short_handed_goal_improvement,¥n        fp.short_handed_assist_improvement¥n    FROM FilteredPerformance fp¥n    INNER JOIN SeasonInfo s ON fp.game_id = s.game_id¥n)¥nSELECT ¥n    fj.game_id,¥n    fj.season_name,¥n    fj.number_of_teams,¥n    fj.goal_improvement,¥n    fj.assist_improvement,¥n    fj.penalty_improvement,¥n    fj.plus_minus_improvement,¥n    fj.time_on_ice,¥n    fj.faceoff_improvement,¥n    fj.hit_improvement,¥n    fj.blocked_shot_improvement,¥n    fj.giveaway_improvement,¥n    fj.takeaway_improvement,¥n    fj.shot_improvement,¥n    fj.power_play_goal_improvement,¥n    fj.power_play_assist_improvement,¥n    fj.short_handed_goal_improvement,¥n    fj.short_handed_assist_improvement¥nFROM FinalJoin fj¥nORDER BY fj.game_id;

question: I want to know the names, total games, average attendance, average time on ice, and average performance of all teams ranked by their average performance and average attendance, along with the names of the users who are authorized to view their data, ordered by the team's rank and the user's level of permission.
cot: Let's break down the task and translate the natural language question into a SQL query. We'll go through it step-by-step, ensuring we encapsulate all the required elements.¥n¥n1. **Understand the Requirements:**¥n   - **Team Information:** Team names, total games played, average attendance, average time on ice, and average performance.¥n   - **User Information:** Names of users authorized to view their data.¥n   - **Ranking:** Teams ranked by average performance and average attendance.¥n   - **Ordering:** Ordered by team's rank and the user's level of permission.¥n¥n2. **Break Down the Query:**¥n   - **TeamGameStats CTE (Common Table Expression):** Calculate total games, average attendance, average time on ice, and average performance for each team.¥n   - **RankedTeamPerformance CTE:** Rank the teams based on average performance and average attendance.¥n   - **UserPermissions CTE:** Extract permissions for users to view data.¥n   - **RecursivePermissions CTE:** This CTE seems unnecessary and may cause confusion. We'll simplify by removing it.¥n   - **Final SELECT Statement:** Join the ranked teams with user permissions and order by team rank and user level (if applicable).¥n¥n3. **Construct the SQL Query:**¥n¥n```sql¥nWITH TeamGameStats AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        t.team_id,¥n        t.team_name,¥n        COUNT(g.game_id) AS total_games,¥n        AVG(g.attendance) AS avg_attendance,¥n        AVG(pg.time_on_ice) AS avg_time_on_ice,¥n        AVG(pg.goals + pg.assists + pg.penalty_minutes) AS avg_performance¥n    FROM teams t¥n    INNER JOIN games g ON t.team_id = g.home_team_id OR t.team_id = g.away_team_id¥n    INNER JOIN player_game_stats pg ON g.game_id = pg.game_id¥n    GROUP BY t.team_id, t.team_name¥n),¥nRankedTeamPerformance AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        tgs.team_id,¥n        tgs.team_name,¥n        tgs.total_games,¥n        tgs.avg_attendance,¥n        tgs.avg_time_on_ice,¥n        tgs.avg_performance,¥n        RANK() OVER (ORDER BY tgs.avg_performance DESC, tgs.avg_attendance DESC) AS team_rank¥n    FROM TeamGameStats tgs¥n),¥nUserPermissions AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        u.user_id,¥n        u.user_name,¥n        json_extract(ur.permissions, '$.view_all_data') AS can_view_all¥n    FROM users u¥n    INNER JOIN user_roles ur ON u.role_id = ur.role_id¥n)¥nSELECT ¥n    rtp.team_id,¥n    rtp.team_name,¥n    rtp.total_games,¥n    rtp.avg_attendance,¥n    rtp.avg_time_on_ice,¥n    rtp.avg_performance,¥n    rtp.team_rank,¥n    up.user_name AS authorized_user¥nFROM RankedTeamPerformance rtp¥nCROSS JOIN UserPermissions up¥nWHERE up.can_view_all = 'true'¥nORDER BY rtp.team_rank, up.user_name;¥n```¥n¥n### Explanation:¥n1. **TeamGameStats CTE:** This CTE calculates the required statistics for each team.¥n2. **RankedTeamPerformance CTE:** This CTE ranks the teams based on performance and attendance.¥n3. **UserPermissions CTE:** This CTE extracts the permissions for users to view data.¥n4. **Final SELECT Statement:** This statement joins the ranked teams with user permissions where the user has permission to view all data. The results are ordered by team rank and user name.¥n¥nThis query ensures that we capture the required information while maintaining clarity and correctness.
sql: WITH TeamGameStats AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        t.team_id,¥n        t.team_name,¥n        COUNT(g.game_id) AS total_games,¥n        AVG(g.attendance) AS avg_attendance,¥n        AVG(pg.time_on_ice) AS avg_time_on_ice,¥n        AVG(pg.goals + pg.assists + pg.penalty_minutes) AS avg_performance¥n    FROM teams t¥n    INNER JOIN games g ON t.team_id = g.home_team_id OR t.team_id = g.away_team_id¥n    INNER JOIN player_game_stats pg ON g.game_id = pg.game_id¥n    GROUP BY t.team_id, t.team_name¥n),¥nRankedTeamPerformance AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        tgs.team_id,¥n        tgs.team_name,¥n        tgs.total_games,¥n        tgs.avg_attendance,¥n        tgs.avg_time_on_ice,¥n        tgs.avg_performance,¥n        RANK() OVER (ORDER BY tgs.avg_performance DESC, tgs.avg_attendance DESC) AS team_rank¥n    FROM TeamGameStats tgs¥n),¥nUserPermissions AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        u.user_id,¥n        u.user_name,¥n        json_extract(ur.permissions, '$.view_all_data') AS can_view_all¥n    FROM users u¥n    INNER JOIN user_roles ur ON u.role_id = ur.role_id¥n)¥nSELECT ¥n    rtp.team_id,¥n    rtp.team_name,¥n    rtp.total_games,¥n    rtp.avg_attendance,¥n    rtp.avg_time_on_ice,¥n    rtp.avg_performance,¥n    rtp.team_rank,¥n    up.user_name AS authorized_user¥nFROM RankedTeamPerformance rtp¥nCROSS JOIN UserPermissions up¥nWHERE up.can_view_all = 'true'¥nORDER BY rtp.team_rank, up.user_name;

question: Find the names of the most valuable players in each game, considering the most valuable player as the one who scored the most points by scoring goals or making assists.
external_knowledge: Most valuable player refers to the player who scored the most points; Points are calculated by adding the number of goals scored and the number of assists made by a player in a game.
cot: To translate the natural language question into an executable SQLite query, let's break down the requirements step-by-step and build a robust query based on the given schema.¥n¥n### Step 1: Understand the Question¥nThe question asks for the names of the most valuable players in each game, defined as the player who scored the most points. Points are calculated by adding the number of goals and assists.¥n¥n### Step 2: Identify Relevant Tables and Columns¥n- `player_game_stats` table contains `game_id`, `player_id`, `goals`, and `assists`.¥n- `players` table contains `player_name` and `player_id`.¥n¥n### Step 3: Calculate Total Points¥nFor each player, calculate the total points as the sum of goals and assists.¥n¥n### Step 4: Rank Players by Total Points in Each Game¥nUse the `ROW_NUMBER()` window function to rank players based on their total points within each game. The player with the highest points gets a rank of 1.¥n¥n### Step 5: Select the Top Player in Each Game¥nFilter the results to only include players with a rank of 1 in each game.¥n¥n### Step 6: Construct the Query¥n¥n#### Step 6.1: Create a Common Table Expression (CTE) to Calculate Points and Rank Players¥n```sql¥nWITH PlayerStatsCTE AS (¥n    SELECT¥n        pgs.game_id,¥n        p.player_name,¥n        pgs.goals + pgs.assists AS total_points,¥n        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY pgs.game_id ORDER BY pgs.goals + pgs.assists DESC) AS player_rank¥n    FROM player_game_stats AS pgs¥n    INNER JOIN players AS p ON pgs.player_id = p.player_id¥n)¥n```¥n¥n- **`INNER JOIN`**: Join `player_game_stats` with `players` to get the player names.¥n- **`ROW_NUMBER()`**: Assign a rank to each player based on their total points within each game.¥n¥n#### Step 6.2: Filter to Get Top Performers¥n```sql¥nSELECT¥n    game_id,¥n    player_name,¥n    total_points¥nFROM PlayerStatsCTE¥nWHERE player_rank = 1¥n```¥n¥n- **`WHERE player_rank = 1`**: Select only the player with the highest points in each game.¥n¥n#### Step 6.3: Final Query to Select Player Names¥n```sql¥nSELECT¥n    tp.player_name AS top_performing_player_in_game¥nFROM TopPerformingPlayers AS tp¥nORDER BY tp.game_id ASC;¥n```¥n¥n- **`ORDER BY tp.game_id ASC`**: Ensure the results are ordered by game ID for clarity.¥n¥n### Final Query¥nCombining all steps, the final query is:¥n¥n```sql¥nWITH PlayerStatsCTE AS (¥n    SELECT¥n        pgs.game_id,¥n        p.player_name,¥n        pgs.goals + pgs.assists AS total_points,¥n        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY pgs.game_id ORDER BY pgs.goals + pgs.assists DESC) AS player_rank¥n    FROM player_game_stats AS pgs¥n    INNER JOIN players AS p ON pgs.player_id = p.player_id¥n),¥nTopPerformingPlayers AS (¥n    SELECT¥n        game_id,¥n        player_name,¥n        total_points¥n    FROM PlayerStatsCTE¥n    WHERE player_rank = 1¥n)¥nSELECT¥n    tp.player_name AS top_performing_player_in_game¥nFROM TopPerformingPlayers AS tp¥nORDER BY tp.game_id ASC;¥n```¥n¥nThis query effectively identifies the most valuable player in each game by calculating total points and ranking players within each game.
sql: WITH PlayerStatsCTE AS (¥n    SELECT¥n        pgs.game_id,¥n        p.player_name,¥n        pgs.goals + pgs.assists AS total_points,¥n        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY pgs.game_id ORDER BY pgs.goals + pgs.assists DESC) AS player_rank¥n    FROM player_game_stats AS pgs¥n    INNER JOIN players AS p ON pgs.player_id = p.player_id¥n),¥nTopPerformingPlayers AS (¥n    SELECT¥n        game_id,¥n        player_name,¥n        total_points¥n    FROM PlayerStatsCTE¥n    WHERE player_rank = 1¥n)¥nSELECT¥n    tp.player_name AS top_performing_player_in_game¥nFROM TopPerformingPlayers AS tp¥nORDER BY tp.game_id ASC;

question: Who are the top 5 players by impact score, along with their respective team names, in the game with game_id 0 and season_id 0?
cot: To translate the natural language question into an executable SQLite query, let's break down the requirements and construct the query step-by-step.¥n¥n### Step-by-Step Reasoning¥n¥n1. **Identify the main goal**: The main goal is to find the top 5 players by impact score in a specific game, along with their respective team names.¥n¥n2. **Define the impact score**: The impact score is calculated based on goals, assists, and blocked shots. The formula provided is ¥¥( ¥¥text{impact score} = ¥¥text{goals} + ¥¥text{assists} + ¥¥text{blocked shots} ¥¥).¥n¥n3. **Determine the relevant tables**: ¥n   - `player_game_stats` to get the game statistics for each player.¥n   - `players` to get the player names.¥n   - `teams` to get the team names.¥n   - `games` to filter by the specific game and season.¥n¥n4. **Filter by game and season**: ¥n   - The game with `game_id = 0` and `season_id = 0` needs to be selected.¥n¥n5. **Calculate the impact score**: ¥n   - A Common Table Expression (CTE) named `PlayerStatsCTE` will be used to calculate the impact score and rank the players.¥n¥n6. **Rank the players**: ¥n   - Use the `RANK()` window function to rank players based on their impact score in descending order.¥n¥n7. **Select the top 5 players**: ¥n   - Filter the results to include only the top 5 players based on their rank.¥n¥n8. **Construct the SQL query**:¥n¥n```sql¥nWITH PlayerStatsCTE AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        p.player_id,¥n        p.player_name,¥n        t.team_name,¥n        pg.goals,¥n        pg.assists,¥n        pg.blocked_shots,¥n        (pg.goals + pg.assists + pg.blocked_shots) AS impact_score,¥n        RANK() OVER (ORDER BY (pg.goals + pg.assists + pg.blocked_shots) DESC) AS rank¥n    FROM player_game_stats pg¥n    INNER JOIN players p ON pg.player_id = p.player_id¥n    INNER JOIN teams t ON p.team_id = t.team_id¥n    INNER JOIN games g ON pg.game_id = g.game_id¥n    WHERE g.season_id = 0 AND g.game_id = 0¥n)¥nSELECT ¥n    ps.player_name,¥n    ps.team_name¥nFROM PlayerStatsCTE ps¥nWHERE ps.rank <= 5;¥n```¥n¥n### Explanation of the Query¥n¥n- **CTE `PlayerStatsCTE`**: ¥n  - Joins `player_game_stats` with `players`, `teams`, and `games` to gather all necessary information.¥n  - Filters the games to only include the game with `game_id = 0` and `season_id = 0`.¥n  - Calculates the impact score and assigns a rank to each player based on their impact score.¥n¥n- **Final Selection**: ¥n  - Selects only the `player_name` and `team_name` from the CTE where the rank is less than or equal to 5.¥n¥nThis query efficiently retrieves the top 5 players by impact score for the specified game and season, along with their respective team names.
sql: WITH PlayerStatsCTE AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        p.player_id,¥n        p.player_name,¥n        t.team_name,¥n        pg.goals,¥n        pg.assists,¥n        pg.blocked_shots,¥n        (pg.goals + pg.assists + pg.blocked_shots) AS impact_score,¥n        RANK() OVER (ORDER BY (pg.goals + pg.assists + pg.blocked_shots) DESC) AS rank¥n    FROM player_game_stats pg¥n    INNER JOIN players p ON pg.player_id = p.player_id¥n    INNER JOIN teams t ON p.team_id = t.team_id¥n    INNER JOIN games g ON pg.game_id = g.game_id¥n    WHERE g.season_id = 0 AND g.game_id = 0¥n)¥nSELECT ¥n    ps.player_name,¥n    ps.team_name¥nFROM PlayerStatsCTE ps¥nWHERE ps.rank <= 5;

question: What are the rankings of players by season based on their total goals and assists, along with their performance statistics and points per game?
external_knowledge: This query assumes that a higher rank is better and that points per game is a meaningful metric for evaluating player performance. The formula for points per game is (total goals + total assists) / total games played.
cot: To translate the natural language question into an executable SQL query, let's break down the requirements and ensure each step is accurately reflected in the query.¥n¥n### Step-by-Step Solution¥n¥n1. **Understand the Task**:¥n   - We need to rank players based on their total goals and assists for each season.¥n   - We need to include performance statistics and calculate points per game.¥n¥n2. **Identify the Relevant Tables**:¥n   - `player_game_stats`: Contains individual game stats for players.¥n   - `games`: Contains game details, including the season.¥n   - `players`: Contains player details.¥n   - `teams`: Contains team details.¥n   - `users` and `user_roles` are not necessary for this task and should be excluded.¥n¥n3. **Calculate Total Goals and Assists**:¥n   - We need to sum up the goals and assists for each player in each season.¥n¥n4. **Calculate Points per Game**:¥n   - The formula given is `(total goals + total assists) / total games played`.¥n   - We need to count the distinct games played by each player in each season.¥n¥n5. **Rank Players**:¥n   - Use the `RANK()` function to rank players based on total goals and assists in descending order.¥n¥n6. **Construct the SQL Query**:¥n   - Use Common Table Expressions (CTEs) to break down the query into manageable parts.¥n¥n### SQL Query Construction¥n¥n```sql¥nWITH GameStatsCTE AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        pgs.player_id,¥n        pgs.game_id,¥n        pgs.goals,¥n        pgs.assists,¥n        pgs.penalty_minutes,¥n        pgs.plus_minus,¥n        pgs.shots_on_goal,¥n        pgs.power_play_goals,¥n        pgs.power_play_assists,¥n        pgs.short_handed_goals,¥n        pgs.short_handed_assists,¥n        g.season_id,¥n        t.team_name,¥n        t.team_abbreviation¥n    FROM player_game_stats pgs¥n    INNER JOIN games g ON pgs.game_id = g.game_id¥n    INNER JOIN players p ON pgs.player_id = p.player_id¥n    INNER JOIN teams t ON p.team_id = t.team_id¥n),¥nTeamPerformanceCTE AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        gs.player_id,¥n        gs.season_id,¥n        SUM(gs.goals) AS total_goals,¥n        SUM(gs.assists) AS total_assists,¥n        SUM(gs.penalty_minutes) AS total_penalty_minutes,¥n        SUM(gs.plus_minus) AS total_plus_minus,¥n        SUM(gs.shots_on_goal) AS total_shots_on_goal,¥n        SUM(gs.power_play_goals) AS total_power_play_goals,¥n        SUM(gs.power_play_assists) AS total_power_play_assists,¥n        SUM(gs.short_handed_goals) AS total_short_handed_goals,¥n        SUM(gs.short_handed_assists) AS total_short_handed_assists,¥n        gs.team_name,¥n        gs.team_abbreviation,¥n        COUNT(DISTINCT gs.game_id) AS total_games_played¥n    FROM GameStatsCTE gs¥n    GROUP BY gs.player_id, gs.season_id, gs.team_name, gs.team_abbreviation¥n),¥nRankedPerformanceCTE AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        tp.player_id,¥n        tp.season_id,¥n        tp.total_goals,¥n        tp.total_assists,¥n        tp.total_penalty_minutes,¥n        tp.total_plus_minus,¥n        tp.total_shots_on_goal,¥n        tp.total_power_play_goals,¥n        tp.total_power_play_assists,¥n        tp.total_short_handed_goals,¥n        tp.total_short_handed_assists,¥n        tp.team_name,¥n        tp.team_abbreviation,¥n        tp.total_games_played,¥n        RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY tp.season_id ORDER BY tp.total_goals DESC, tp.total_assists DESC) AS rank¥n    FROM TeamPerformanceCTE tp¥n)¥nSELECT ¥n    rp.player_id,¥n    rp.season_id,¥n    rp.total_goals,¥n    rp.total_assists,¥n    rp.total_penalty_minutes,¥n    rp.total_plus_minus,¥n    rp.total_shots_on_goal,¥n    rp.total_power_play_goals,¥n    rp.total_power_play_assists,¥n    rp.total_short_handed_goals,¥n    rp.total_short_handed_assists,¥n    rp.team_name,¥n    rp.team_abbreviation,¥n    rp.total_games_played,¥n    rp.rank,¥n    ROUND((rp.total_goals + rp.total_assists) / CAST(NULLIF(rp.total_games_played, 0) AS REAL), 2) AS points_per_game¥nFROM RankedPerformanceCTE rp¥nORDER BY rp.season_id, rp.rank;¥n```¥n¥n### Explanation of the Query¥n¥n- **GameStatsCTE**: Extracts relevant player game statistics along with the season and team details.¥n- **TeamPerformanceCTE**: Aggregates total goals, assists, and other performance metrics for each player in each season. It also counts the distinct games played.¥n- **RankedPerformanceCTE**: Ranks players within each season based on their total goals and assists.¥n- **Final SELECT**: Retrieves all necessary data and calculates the points per game, ensuring division by zero is handled with `NULLIF`.¥n¥nThis query should meet the requirements specified in the natural language question and efficiently calculate the desired rankings and statistics.
sql: WITH GameStatsCTE AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        pgs.player_id,¥n        pgs.game_id,¥n        pgs.goals,¥n        pgs.assists,¥n        pgs.penalty_minutes,¥n        pgs.plus_minus,¥n        pgs.shots_on_goal,¥n        pgs.power_play_goals,¥n        pgs.power_play_assists,¥n        pgs.short_handed_goals,¥n        pgs.short_handed_assists,¥n        g.season_id,¥n        t.team_name,¥n        t.team_abbreviation¥n    FROM player_game_stats pgs¥n    INNER JOIN games g ON pgs.game_id = g.game_id¥n    INNER JOIN players p ON pgs.player_id = p.player_id¥n    INNER JOIN teams t ON p.team_id = t.team_id¥n),¥nTeamPerformanceCTE AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        gs.player_id,¥n        gs.season_id,¥n        SUM(gs.goals) AS total_goals,¥n        SUM(gs.assists) AS total_assists,¥n        SUM(gs.penalty_minutes) AS total_penalty_minutes,¥n        SUM(gs.plus_minus) AS total_plus_minus,¥n        SUM(gs.shots_on_goal) AS total_shots_on_goal,¥n        SUM(gs.power_play_goals) AS total_power_play_goals,¥n        SUM(gs.power_play_assists) AS total_power_play_assists,¥n        SUM(gs.short_handed_goals) AS total_short_handed_goals,¥n        SUM(gs.short_handed_assists) AS total_short_handed_assists,¥n        gs.team_name,¥n        gs.team_abbreviation,¥n        COUNT(DISTINCT gs.game_id) AS total_games_played¥n    FROM GameStatsCTE gs¥n    GROUP BY gs.player_id, gs.season_id, gs.team_name, gs.team_abbreviation¥n),¥nRankedPerformanceCTE AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        tp.player_id,¥n        tp.season_id,¥n        tp.total_goals,¥n        tp.total_assists,¥n        tp.total_penalty_minutes,¥n        tp.total_plus_minus,¥n        tp.total_shots_on_goal,¥n        tp.total_power_play_goals,¥n        tp.total_power_play_assists,¥n        tp.total_short_handed_goals,¥n        tp.total_short_handed_assists,¥n        tp.team_name,¥n        tp.team_abbreviation,¥n        tp.total_games_played,¥n        RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY tp.season_id ORDER BY tp.total_goals DESC, tp.total_assists DESC) AS rank¥n    FROM TeamPerformanceCTE tp¥n)¥nSELECT ¥n    rp.player_id,¥n    rp.season_id,¥n    rp.total_goals,¥n    rp.total_assists,¥n    rp.total_penalty_minutes,¥n    rp.total_plus_minus,¥n    rp.total_shots_on_goal,¥n    rp.total_power_play_goals,¥n    rp.total_power_play_assists,¥n    rp.total_short_handed_goals,¥n    rp.total_short_handed_assists,¥n    rp.team_name,¥n    rp.team_abbreviation,¥n    rp.total_games_played,¥n    rp.rank,¥n    ROUND((rp.total_goals + rp.total_assists) / CAST(NULLIF(rp.total_games_played, 0) AS REAL), 2) AS points_per_game¥nFROM RankedPerformanceCTE rp¥nORDER BY rp.season_id, rp.rank;

question: **User**: I'm looking for some information on top-performing players for a specific season.¥n**Assistant**: Which season are you interested in?¥n**User**: I'm looking at the 2009 season.¥n**Assistant**: What kind of performance are you looking to evaluate?¥n**User**: I want to rank them based on the number of goals and assists they made.¥n**Assistant**: Would you like to consider all players or just those with the highest number of goals and assists?¥n**User**: Just the players who scored the most goals and assists.¥n**Assistant**: What information would you like to know about these top players?¥n**User**: Their names and total goals.¥n**Assistant**: Is there anything else you'd like to know?¥n**User**: No, that's all.¥n**Assistant**: Alright, I'll translate your query into an SQL query for you.
cot: Let's break down the task to translate the natural language question into an SQL query step by step:¥n¥n1. **Understand the Question**:¥n   - The user wants information on top-performing players for the 2009 season.¥n   - Performance is evaluated based on the number of goals and assists.¥n   - Only the players with the highest number of goals and assists should be considered.¥n   - The desired information includes players' names and their total goals.¥n¥n2. **Identify Relevant Tables**:¥n   - `players`: Contains player details.¥n   - `player_game_stats`: Contains statistics for each player per game.¥n   - `games`: Contains game details, which includes the season ID.¥n   - `seasons`: Contains season details, specifically the season name.¥n¥n3. **Formulate the SQL Query**:¥n   - **Join Tables**: We need to join `players`, `player_game_stats`, `games`, and `seasons` to gather all necessary information.¥n   - **Filter by Season**: We need to filter the records to only include those from the 2009 season.¥n   - **Aggregate Data**: Sum up the goals and assists for each player.¥n   - **Rank Players**: Rank players based on the total goals and assists.¥n   - **Select Top Players**: Select only those players who rank at the top in terms of goals and assists.¥n¥n4. **Construct the Query**:¥n   - First, we join the tables to get the necessary data.¥n   - Next, we filter the data for the 2009 season.¥n   - We then calculate the total goals and assists for each player.¥n   - After that, we rank the players based on these totals.¥n   - Finally, we select the top players and their total goals.¥n¥nHere's the SQL query that we derive:¥n¥n```sql¥nWITH PlayerSeasonStats AS (¥n    SELECT¥n        p.player_id,¥n        p.player_name,¥n        SUM(pg.goals) AS total_goals,¥n        SUM(pg.assists) AS total_assists¥n    FROM¥n        players p¥n    JOIN¥n        player_game_stats pg ON p.player_id = pg.player_id¥n    JOIN¥n        games g ON pg.game_id = g.game_id¥n    JOIN¥n        seasons s ON g.season_id = s.season_id¥n    WHERE¥n        s.season_name = '2009'¥n    GROUP BY¥n        p.player_id, p.player_name¥n),¥nRankedPlayers AS (¥n    SELECT¥n        player_id,¥n        player_name,¥n        total_goals,¥n        total_assists,¥n        RANK() OVER (¥n            ORDER BY total_goals DESC, total_assists DESC¥n        ) AS player_rank¥n    FROM¥n        PlayerSeasonStats¥n)¥nSELECT¥n    player_name,¥n    total_goals¥nFROM¥n    RankedPlayers¥nWHERE¥n    player_rank = 1¥nORDER BY¥n    total_goals DESC;¥n```¥n¥n### Explanation:¥n1. **PlayerSeasonStats CTE**:¥n   - Joins `players`, `player_game_stats`, `games`, and `seasons`.¥n   - Filters for games in the 2009 season.¥n   - Aggregates the total goals and assists for each player.¥n¥n2. **RankedPlayers CTE**:¥n   - Ranks players based on their total goals and assists in descending order.¥n¥n3. **Final SELECT**:¥n   - Selects the top-ranked players (those with rank 1) and their total goals.¥n   - Orders the results by total goals in descending order.
sql: WITH PlayerSeasonStats AS (¥n    SELECT¥n        p.player_id,¥n        p.player_name,¥n        SUM(pg.goals) AS total_goals,¥n        SUM(pg.assists) AS total_assists¥n    FROM¥n        players p¥n    JOIN¥n        player_game_stats pg ON p.player_id = pg.player_id¥n    JOIN¥n        games g ON pg.game_id = g.game_id¥n    JOIN¥n        seasons s ON g.season_id = s.season_id¥n    WHERE¥n        s.season_name = '2009'¥n    GROUP BY¥n        p.player_id, p.player_name¥n),¥nRankedPlayers AS (¥n    SELECT¥n        player_id,¥n        player_name,¥n        total_goals,¥n        total_assists,¥n        RANK() OVER (¥n            ORDER BY total_goals DESC, total_assists DESC¥n        ) AS player_rank¥n    FROM¥n        PlayerSeasonStats¥n)¥nSELECT¥n    player_name,¥n    total_goals¥nFROM¥n    RankedPlayers¥nWHERE¥n    player_rank = 1¥nORDER BY¥n    total_goals DESC;

question: Hey, could you show me a list of all the seasons along with their average attendance, total number of games played, the team that scored the most goals in each season, and the average number of goals scored per game by that team? I'd love to see this information ordered by season.
cot: To translate the given narrative question into an SQL query, we will follow a structured approach. The task requires us to gather data about each season, including:¥n¥n1. The season name.¥n2. The average attendance across all games in each season.¥n3. The total number of games played in each season.¥n4. The team that scored the most goals in each season.¥n5. The average number of goals scored per game by that top-scoring team.¥n¥nLet's break down the steps required to derive the SQL query:¥n¥n### Step 1: Retrieve Season Information¥nFirst, we need to fetch the season names from the `seasons` table.¥n¥n### Step 2: Calculate Average Attendance and Total Games¥nWe will create a Common Table Expression (CTE) named `SeasonStats` to calculate the average attendance and total number of games for each season.¥n¥n### Step 3: Identify Top Scoring Teams¥nNext, we need to determine the top scoring team for each season. This involves:¥n- Joining `player_game_stats` with `games` to get the goals scored by each team in each game.¥n- Summing the goals for each team per season.¥n- Identifying the top scoring team by ranking based on total goals.¥n¥n### Step 4: Calculate Average Goals Per Game by Top Scoring Team¥nFinally, we need to calculate the average number of goals scored per game by the top scoring team in each season.¥n¥n### Step 5: Combine All Data and Order¥nWe will combine all the above data into a single result set and order it by season name.¥n¥nLet's construct the SQL query based on the above steps:¥n¥n```sql¥nWITH SeasonStats AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        s.season_id,¥n        s.season_name,¥n        AVG(g.attendance) AS average_attendance,¥n        COUNT(g.game_id) AS total_games¥n    FROM seasons s¥n    JOIN games g ON s.season_id = g.season_id¥n    GROUP BY s.season_id, s.season_name¥n),¥nTopScoringTeams AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        season_id,¥n        team_id,¥n        SUM(goals) AS total_goals¥n    FROM (¥n        SELECT ¥n            g.season_id,¥n            g.home_team_id AS team_id,¥n            pgs.goals¥n        FROM games g¥n        JOIN player_game_stats pgs ON g.game_id = pgs.game_id¥n        UNION ALL¥n        SELECT ¥n            g.season_id,¥n            g.away_team_id AS team_id,¥n            pgs.goals¥n        FROM games g¥n        JOIN player_game_stats pgs ON g.game_id = pgs.game_id¥n    ) AS team_goals¥n    GROUP BY season_id, team_id¥n),¥nRankedTeams AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        season_id,¥n        team_id,¥n        total_goals,¥n        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY season_id ORDER BY total_goals DESC) AS rank¥n    FROM TopScoringTeams¥n),¥nTopTeamStats AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        rt.season_id,¥n        rt.team_id,¥n        t.team_name,¥n        AVG(pgs.goals) AS avg_goals_per_game¥n    FROM RankedTeams rt¥n    JOIN teams t ON rt.team_id = t.team_id¥n    JOIN player_game_stats pgs ON pgs.player_id IN (¥n        SELECT player_id FROM player_game_stats WHERE game_id IN (¥n            SELECT game_id FROM games WHERE season_id = rt.season_id AND (home_team_id = rt.team_id OR away_team_id = rt.team_id)¥n        )¥n    )¥n    WHERE rt.rank = 1¥n    GROUP BY rt.season_id, rt.team_id, t.team_name¥n)¥nSELECT ¥n    ss.season_name,¥n    ss.average_attendance,¥n    ss.total_games,¥n    tts.team_name AS top_scoring_team,¥n    tts.avg_goals_per_game¥nFROM SeasonStats ss¥nLEFT JOIN TopTeamStats tts ON ss.season_id = tts.season_id¥nORDER BY ss.season_name;¥n```¥n¥nThis query should provide the desired information, including the season name, average attendance, total number of games, the top scoring team, and the average number of goals scored per game by that team, all ordered by season name.
sql: WITH SeasonStats AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        s.season_id,¥n        s.season_name,¥n        AVG(g.attendance) AS average_attendance,¥n        COUNT(g.game_id) AS total_games¥n    FROM seasons s¥n    JOIN games g ON s.season_id = g.season_id¥n    GROUP BY s.season_id, s.season_name¥n),¥nTopScoringTeams AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        season_id,¥n        team_id,¥n        SUM(goals) AS total_goals¥n    FROM (¥n        SELECT ¥n            g.season_id,¥n            g.home_team_id AS team_id,¥n            pgs.goals¥n        FROM games g¥n        JOIN player_game_stats pgs ON g.game_id = pgs.game_id¥n        UNION ALL¥n        SELECT ¥n            g.season_id,¥n            g.away_team_id AS team_id,¥n            pgs.goals¥n        FROM games g¥n        JOIN player_game_stats pgs ON g.game_id = pgs.game_id¥n    ) AS team_goals¥n    GROUP BY season_id, team_id¥n),¥nRankedTeams AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        season_id,¥n        team_id,¥n        total_goals,¥n        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY season_id ORDER BY total_goals DESC) AS rank¥n    FROM TopScoringTeams¥n),¥nTopTeamStats AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        rt.season_id,¥n        rt.team_id,¥n        t.team_name,¥n        AVG(pgs.goals) AS avg_goals_per_game¥n    FROM RankedTeams rt¥n    JOIN teams t ON rt.team_id = t.team_id¥n    JOIN player_game_stats pgs ON pgs.player_id IN (¥n        SELECT player_id FROM player_game_stats WHERE game_id IN (¥n            SELECT game_id FROM games WHERE season_id = rt.season_id AND (home_team_id = rt.team_id OR away_team_id = rt.team_id)¥n        )¥n    )¥n    WHERE rt.rank = 1¥n    GROUP BY rt.season_id, rt.team_id, t.team_name¥n)¥nSELECT ¥n    ss.season_name,¥n    ss.average_attendance,¥n    ss.total_games,¥n    tts.team_name AS top_scoring_team,¥n    tts.avg_goals_per_game¥nFROM SeasonStats ss¥nLEFT JOIN TopTeamStats tts ON ss.season_id = tts.season_id¥nORDER BY ss.season_name;

question: Who is the player with the highest total goals scored in season 0, and what is the name of their team?
cot: To determine the player with the highest total goals scored in season 0 and the name of their team, we can break down the task into several steps:¥n¥n1. **Identify relevant tables**: We need to access the `players`, `player_game_stats`, `games`, and `teams` tables to gather the necessary information.¥n¥n2. **Join tables**: We will join `players` with `player_game_stats` on `player_id`, and then join `player_game_stats` with `games` on `game_id` to filter by season. Finally, we join `players` with `teams` on `team_id` to get the team name.¥n¥n3. **Filter by season**: We will use the `WHERE` clause to filter the games to only those that occurred in season 0.¥n¥n4. **Calculate total goals**: We will use the `SUM` function to calculate the total goals scored by each player.¥n¥n5. **Rank players by goals**: We will use the `ROW_NUMBER` window function to rank players based on their total goals in descending order.¥n¥n6. **Select the top player**: We will select the player with the highest total goals (rank 1).¥n¥nHere is the step-by-step SQL query:¥n¥n```sql¥n-- Step 1: Join players with player_game_stats and games to filter by season¥nWITH PlayerGoalsCTE AS (¥n    SELECT¥n        p.player_id,¥n        p.player_name,¥n        p.team_id,¥n        SUM(pg.goals) AS total_goals¥n    FROM¥n        players p¥n    JOIN¥n        player_game_stats pg ON p.player_id = pg.player_id¥n    JOIN¥n        games g ON pg.game_id = g.game_id¥n    WHERE¥n        g.season_id = 0¥n    GROUP BY¥n        p.player_id, p.player_name, p.team_id¥n),¥n-- Step 2: Rank players by total goals¥nRankedPlayers AS (¥n    SELECT¥n        pg.player_id,¥n        pg.player_name,¥n        pg.team_id,¥n        pg.total_goals,¥n        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY pg.total_goals DESC) AS goal_rank¥n    FROM¥n        PlayerGoalsCTE pg¥n)¥n-- Step 3: Select the top player and their team name¥nSELECT¥n    rp.player_name,¥n    t.team_name¥nFROM¥n    RankedPlayers rp¥nJOIN¥n    teams t ON rp.team_id = t.team_id¥nWHERE¥n    rp.goal_rank = 1;¥n```¥n¥n### Explanation:¥n¥n- **PlayerGoalsCTE**: This Common Table Expression (CTE) calculates the total goals scored by each player in season 0. It joins `players`, `player_game_stats`, and `games` tables, filters by season, and groups by player.¥n¥n- **RankedPlayers**: This CTE ranks the players based on their total goals using the `ROW_NUMBER` window function.¥n¥n- **Final SELECT**: This query selects the player with the highest goals (rank = 1) and joins with the `teams` table to get the team name.¥n¥nThis query is efficient and directly addresses the question by filtering, calculating, and ranking the necessary data.
sql: -- Step 1: Join players with player_game_stats and games to filter by season¥nWITH PlayerGoalsCTE AS (¥n    SELECT¥n        p.player_id,¥n        p.player_name,¥n        p.team_id,¥n        SUM(pg.goals) AS total_goals¥n    FROM¥n        players p¥n    JOIN¥n        player_game_stats pg ON p.player_id = pg.player_id¥n    JOIN¥n        games g ON pg.game_id = g.game_id¥n    WHERE¥n        g.season_id = 0¥n    GROUP BY¥n        p.player_id, p.player_name, p.team_id¥n),¥n-- Step 2: Rank players by total goals¥nRankedPlayers AS (¥n    SELECT¥n        pg.player_id,¥n        pg.player_name,¥n        pg.team_id,¥n        pg.total_goals,¥n        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY pg.total_goals DESC) AS goal_rank¥n    FROM¥n        PlayerGoalsCTE pg¥n)¥n-- Step 3: Select the top player and their team name¥nSELECT¥n    rp.player_name,¥n    t.team_name¥nFROM¥n    RankedPlayers rp¥nJOIN¥n    teams t ON rp.team_id = t.team_id¥nWHERE¥n    rp.goal_rank = 1;

question: Find the names of teams that won championships in the 2009 season, along with the number of championships they won and the total number of games they played, and return the results sorted by the number of championships won in descending order, and then by the total number of games played in descending order.
cot: To translate the given natural language question into an executable SQLite query, we need to break down the question and determine the necessary steps to retrieve the required data. Let's go through the process step-by-step:¥n¥n1. **Identify the required data**:¥n   - Team names¥n   - Number of championships won in the 2009 season¥n   - Total number of games played by these teams¥n¥n2. **Determine the tables involved**:¥n   - `teams`: To get the team names.¥n   - `seasons`: To filter seasons by the year 2009 and get the champion team IDs.¥n   - `games`: To count the total number of games played by each team.¥n¥n3. **Formulate the query**:¥n   - **Join `teams` with `seasons`**: This will allow us to filter teams that won championships in the 2009 season.¥n   - **Count the number of championships**: Use `COUNT` to determine how many times each team was the champion in 2009.¥n   - **Count the total number of games played**: Use a subquery to count the total number of games where each team was involved (either as the home team or the away team).¥n   - **Filter by the 2009 season**: Use the `WHERE` clause to filter the results to only include the 2009 season.¥n   - **Group by team**: Use `GROUP BY` to aggregate the results by each team.¥n   - **Order the results**: Use `ORDER BY` to sort the results first by the number of championships won in descending order, and then by the total number of games played in descending order.¥n¥n4. **Construct the SQL query**:¥n   - Start with the `SELECT` statement to specify the required columns.¥n   - Use `JOIN` to connect the `teams` table with the `seasons` table.¥n   - Use a subquery to count the total number of games played by each team.¥n   - Apply the `WHERE` clause to filter by the 2009 season.¥n   - Use `GROUP BY` to group the results by team.¥n   - Use `HAVING` to ensure that only teams with at least one championship win are included.¥n   - Finally, use `ORDER BY` to sort the results as required.¥n¥nHere is the final SQL query:¥n¥n```sql¥nSELECT t.team_name, ¥n       COUNT(s.season_id) AS championship_wins, ¥n       (SELECT COUNT(*) ¥n        FROM games g ¥n        WHERE g.home_team_id = t.team_id OR g.away_team_id = t.team_id) AS total_games_played¥nFROM teams t¥nLEFT JOIN seasons s ON t.team_id = s.champion_team_id¥nWHERE s.season_name = '2009'¥nGROUP BY t.team_id, t.team_name¥nHAVING championship_wins > 0¥nORDER BY championship_wins DESC, total_games_played DESC;¥n```¥n¥nThis query should correctly retrieve the names of teams that won championships in the 2009 season, along with the number of championships they won and the total number of games they played, sorted by the number of championships won in descending order, and then by the total number of games played in descending order.
sql: SELECT t.team_name, ¥n       COUNT(s.season_id) AS championship_wins, ¥n       (SELECT COUNT(*) ¥n        FROM games g ¥n        WHERE g.home_team_id = t.team_id OR g.away_team_id = t.team_id) AS total_games_played¥nFROM teams t¥nLEFT JOIN seasons s ON t.team_id = s.champion_team_id¥nWHERE s.season_name = '2009'¥nGROUP BY t.team_id, t.team_name¥nHAVING championship_wins > 0¥nORDER BY championship_wins DESC, total_games_played DESC;

question: Can you provide me with a list of the names of top-performing players who played in clear weather conditions and whose teams won the championship in that season, considering each player's top 5 game performances based on goals scored and assists?
cot: To translate the natural language question into an executable SQLite query, we need to break down the requirements and ensure that the query accurately reflects the conditions specified in the question. Here's a step-by-step explanation of how to derive the query:¥n¥n### Step-by-Step Solution¥n¥n1. **Identify the main entities involved**:¥n   - Players¥n   - Game statistics¥n   - Games¥n   - Seasons¥n   - Teams¥n¥n2. **Understand the conditions**:¥n   - The games should be played in clear weather conditions.¥n   - The player's team should have won the championship in the season.¥n   - We need to consider each player's top 5 game performances based on goals scored and assists.¥n¥n3. **Determine the required columns**:¥n   - Player name¥n   - Season name¥n   - Date of the game¥n   - Champion team ID¥n   - Team name¥n   - City¥n   - Weather¥n   - Goals¥n   - Assists¥n¥n4. **Create Common Table Expressions (CTEs)**:¥n   - **PlayerGameStatsCTE**: This CTE will join the necessary tables to get the required data.¥n   - **RankedPlayersCTE**: This CTE will rank the player's game performances based on goals and assists.¥n¥n5. **Construct the query**:¥n   - Start by selecting the necessary columns from the joined tables.¥n   - Apply the conditions for clear weather and championship-winning teams.¥n   - Rank the player's game performances and filter to get the top 5.¥n¥n### Constructing the SQL Query¥n¥n```sql¥nWITH PlayerGameStatsCTE AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        p.player_id, ¥n        p.player_name, ¥n        g.season_id, ¥n        s.season_name, ¥n        g.date,¥n        s.champion_team_id,¥n        t.team_name,¥n        t.city,¥n        g.weather,¥n        ps.goals,¥n        ps.assists,¥n        ps.penalty_minutes¥n    FROM ¥n        players p¥n    JOIN ¥n        player_game_stats ps ON p.player_id = ps.player_id¥n    JOIN ¥n        games g ON ps.game_id = g.game_id¥n    JOIN ¥n        seasons s ON g.season_id = s.season_id¥n    JOIN ¥n        teams t ON p.team_id = t.team_id¥n    WHERE ¥n        g.weather = 'clear'¥n        AND s.champion_team_id = t.team_id¥n),¥nRankedPlayersCTE AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        player_id,¥n        player_name,¥n        season_id,¥n        season_name,¥n        date,¥n        champion_team_id,¥n        team_name,¥n        city,¥n        weather,¥n        goals,¥n        assists,¥n        penalty_minutes,¥n        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY goals DESC, assists DESC) as goal_assist_rank¥n    FROM ¥n        PlayerGameStatsCTE¥n)¥nSELECT ¥n    DISTINCT player_name¥nFROM ¥n    RankedPlayersCTE¥nWHERE ¥n    goal_assist_rank <= 5¥nORDER BY ¥n    player_name;¥n```¥n¥n### Explanation of the Query¥n¥n1. **PlayerGameStatsCTE**:¥n   - Joins the `players`, `player_game_stats`, `games`, `seasons`, and `teams` tables to get the required data.¥n   - Filters for games played in clear weather conditions.¥n   - Ensures that the team's ID in the `players` table matches the champion team's ID in the `seasons` table.¥n¥n2. **RankedPlayersCTE**:¥n   - Ranks the player's game performances based on goals and assists using the `ROW_NUMBER()` window function.¥n   - Partitions the results by `player_id` to rank each player's games separately.¥n¥n3. **Final Selection**:¥n   - Selects distinct player names from the `RankedPlayersCTE` where the rank is less than or equal to 5.¥n   - Orders the results by player name for readability.¥n¥nThis query ensures that we get the list of top-performing players who played in clear weather conditions and whose teams won the championship in that season, considering each player's top 5 game performances based on goals scored and assists.
sql: WITH PlayerGameStatsCTE AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        p.player_id, ¥n        p.player_name, ¥n        g.season_id, ¥n        s.season_name, ¥n        g.date,¥n        s.champion_team_id,¥n        t.team_name,¥n        t.city,¥n        g.weather,¥n        ps.goals,¥n        ps.assists,¥n        ps.penalty_minutes¥n    FROM ¥n        players p¥n    JOIN ¥n        player_game_stats ps ON p.player_id = ps.player_id¥n    JOIN ¥n        games g ON ps.game_id = g.game_id¥n    JOIN ¥n        seasons s ON g.season_id = s.season_id¥n    JOIN ¥n        teams t ON p.team_id = t.team_id¥n    WHERE ¥n        g.weather = 'clear'¥n        AND s.champion_team_id = t.team_id¥n),¥nRankedPlayersCTE AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        player_id,¥n        player_name,¥n        season_id,¥n        season_name,¥n        date,¥n        champion_team_id,¥n        team_name,¥n        city,¥n        weather,¥n        goals,¥n        assists,¥n        penalty_minutes,¥n        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY goals DESC, assists DESC) as goal_assist_rank¥n    FROM ¥n        PlayerGameStatsCTE¥n)¥nSELECT ¥n    DISTINCT player_name¥nFROM ¥n    RankedPlayersCTE¥nWHERE ¥n    goal_assist_rank <= 5¥nORDER BY ¥n    player_name;

question: Find the names of the top-performing players in each season based on their average points per game.
cot: To translate the natural language question into a correct and efficient SQL query, we'll break down the problem and construct the query step by step. The goal is to identify the top-performing players in each season based on their average points per game.¥n¥n### Step-by-Step Solution¥n¥n1. **Identify Relevant Tables**: We need data from `player_game_stats` (for game statistics), `players` (for player names), and `games` (for season information).¥n¥n2. **Calculate Total Points and Games Played**: We need to sum up the goals and assists for each player in each game and count the number of games they played.¥n¥n3. **Calculate Average Points per Game**: Divide the total points by the number of games played to get the average points per game.¥n¥n4. **Rank Players within Each Season**: Use the `RANK()` window function to rank players based on their average points per game within each season.¥n¥n5. **Select Top Performers**: Filter to get only the top-ranked player (rank = 1) for each season.¥n¥n### Constructing the Query¥n¥n1. **Join Tables**: Start by joining `player_game_stats`, `games`, and `players` to get the necessary data.¥n¥n2. **Aggregate Data**: Use `SUM` to calculate total points and `COUNT` to determine the number of games played.¥n¥n3. **Compute Average Points per Game**: Divide the total points by the number of games played.¥n¥n4. **Rank Players**: Use `RANK()` over each season to rank players based on their average points per game.¥n¥n5. **Filter Top Performers**: Select players with rank 1.¥n¥n### SQL Query¥n¥n```sql¥nWITH PlayerPerformanceCTE AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        pg.player_id,¥n        p.player_name,¥n        g.season_id,¥n        SUM(pg.goals + pg.assists) AS total_points,¥n        COUNT(g.game_id) AS games_played,¥n        SUM(pg.goals + pg.assists) / COUNT(g.game_id) AS avg_points_per_game¥n    FROM ¥n        player_game_stats pg¥n    JOIN ¥n        games g ON pg.game_id = g.game_id¥n    JOIN ¥n        players p ON pg.player_id = p.player_id¥n    GROUP BY ¥n        pg.player_id, g.season_id¥n),¥nRankedPerformanceCTE AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        player_name,¥n        season_id,¥n        avg_points_per_game,¥n        RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY season_id ORDER BY avg_points_per_game DESC) AS rank¥n    FROM ¥n        PlayerPerformanceCTE¥n)¥nSELECT ¥n    player_name,¥n    season_id¥nFROM ¥n    RankedPerformanceCTE¥nWHERE ¥n    rank = 1;¥n```¥n¥n### Explanation¥n¥n- **PlayerPerformanceCTE**: This common table expression (CTE) calculates the total points and average points per game for each player in each season.¥n  ¥n- **RankedPerformanceCTE**: This CTE ranks players within each season based on their average points per game.¥n¥n- **Final SELECT**: We select the player names and their corresponding seasons where they are ranked as the top performer (rank = 1).¥n¥nThis query efficiently answers the question by leveraging SQL's powerful aggregation and window functions to compute and rank the top performers in each season.
sql: WITH PlayerPerformanceCTE AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        pg.player_id,¥n        p.player_name,¥n        g.season_id,¥n        SUM(pg.goals + pg.assists) AS total_points,¥n        COUNT(g.game_id) AS games_played,¥n        SUM(pg.goals + pg.assists) / COUNT(g.game_id) AS avg_points_per_game¥n    FROM ¥n        player_game_stats pg¥n    JOIN ¥n        games g ON pg.game_id = g.game_id¥n    JOIN ¥n        players p ON pg.player_id = p.player_id¥n    GROUP BY ¥n        pg.player_id, g.season_id¥n),¥nRankedPerformanceCTE AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        player_name,¥n        season_id,¥n        avg_points_per_game,¥n        RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY season_id ORDER BY avg_points_per_game DESC) AS rank¥n    FROM ¥n        PlayerPerformanceCTE¥n)¥nSELECT ¥n    player_name,¥n    season_id¥nFROM ¥n    RankedPerformanceCTE¥nWHERE ¥n    rank = 1;

question: What are the names and performance details of players who have had notable games, where they scored multiple goals or had many assists, ordered by the most impressive performance?
external_knowledge: Multiple goals means more than one goal; many assists means more than two assists; Notable games refer to the top performances; Impressive performance refers to the combination of goals and assists.
cot: To translate the natural language question into an executable SQLite query, let's break down the requirements and construct the query step-by-step.¥n¥n### Step 1: Understand the Requirements¥nThe question asks for players who have had notable games, defined as games where they scored multiple goals (more than one goal) or had many assists (more than two assists). We need to retrieve the player's name, game details (date and location), and performance details (goals, assists, time on ice, and plus-minus rating). The results should be ordered by the most impressive performance, which is determined by the combination of goals and assists.¥n¥n### Step 2: Identify Relevant Tables and Columns¥n- **`player_game_stats`**: Contains performance details such as goals, assists, time on ice, and plus-minus rating.¥n- **`players`**: Contains player names.¥n- **`games`**: Contains game details such as date and location.¥n¥n### Step 3: Construct the Query¥n1. **SELECT Clause**: We need to select the player's name, game date, game location, goals, assists, time on ice, and plus-minus rating.¥n2. **FROM Clause**: We will start from the `player_game_stats` table and join it with the `players` and `games` tables to get the required information.¥n3. **JOIN Clauses**: ¥n   - Join `players` on `player_id` to get the player's name.¥n   - Join `games` on `game_id` to get the game details.¥n4. **WHERE Clause**: Filter the results to include only those games where the player scored more than one goal or had more than two assists.¥n5. **ORDER BY Clause**: Order the results by the number of goals in descending order and then by the number of assists in descending order to determine the most impressive performance.¥n¥n### Step 4: Write the SQL Query¥nBased on the above steps, the SQL query can be written as follows:¥n¥n```sql¥nSELECT ¥n  p.player_name,¥n  g.date AS game_date,¥n  g.location AS game_location,¥n  pgs.goals,¥n  pgs.assists,¥n  pgs.time_on_ice,¥n  pgs.plus_minus¥nFROM ¥n  player_game_stats pgs¥nJOIN ¥n  players p ON pgs.player_id = p.player_id¥nJOIN ¥n  games g ON pgs.game_id = g.game_id¥nWHERE ¥n  pgs.goals > 1 OR pgs.assists > 2¥nORDER BY ¥n  pgs.goals DESC, pgs.assists DESC;¥n```¥n¥n### Step 5: Verify the Query¥n- The query correctly joins the necessary tables.¥n- It filters the results based on the criteria provided (multiple goals or many assists).¥n- It orders the results by the most impressive performance, first by goals and then by assists.¥n- There are no unnecessary table or column selections, and the joins are appropriate for the given requirements.¥n¥nThis query should provide the desired results as specified in the natural language question.
sql: SELECT ¥n  p.player_name,¥n  g.date AS game_date,¥n  g.location AS game_location,¥n  pgs.goals,¥n  pgs.assists,¥n  pgs.time_on_ice,¥n  pgs.plus_minus¥nFROM ¥n  player_game_stats pgs¥nJOIN ¥n  players p ON pgs.player_id = p.player_id¥nJOIN ¥n  games g ON pgs.game_id = g.game_id¥nWHERE ¥n  pgs.goals > 1 OR pgs.assists > 2¥nORDER BY ¥n  pgs.goals DESC, pgs.assists DESC;

question: **User**: I want to get some information about player performance.¥n**Assistant**: What kind of information would you like to know about the players?¥n**User**: I'm interested in their average time on ice.¥n**Assistant**: Do you want to consider all players or those who have played a certain number of games?¥n**User**: Only players who have played more than 10 games.¥n**Assistant**: That's a good filter. Is there a minimum average time on ice you're interested in?¥n**User**: Yes, I want players with an average time on ice of more than 15 minutes.¥n**Assistant**: So, you want the average time on ice for players who have played more than 10 games and have an average time on ice greater than 15 minutes. Would you like the list sorted by their average time on ice?¥n**User**: Yes, please sort it in descending order.¥n**Assistant**: Last question, do you want any other information about these players besides their names and average time on ice?¥n**User**: No, just those two pieces of information are fine.¥n**Assistant**: Alright, I'll help you with that. I'll translate your request into an SQL query for you.
cot: To translate the natural language question into an executable SQLite query, let's break down the requirements and construct the query step-by-step:¥n¥n### Step-by-Step Solution:¥n¥n1. **Identify the Tables Involved**:¥n   - We need data from the `players` table to get player names.¥n   - We need data from the `player_game_stats` table to get the time on ice for each player.¥n¥n2. **Join the Tables**:¥n   - We will join the `players` table (`p`) with the `player_game_stats` table (`pg`) on the `player_id` column to link each player with their game statistics.¥n¥n3. **Filter Conditions**:¥n   - We need to filter players who have played more than 10 games. This can be done using the `HAVING` clause on the count of `game_id`.¥n   - We also need to filter players whose average time on ice is more than 15 minutes. Since `time_on_ice` is stored as a string in the format ¥

, we need to handle this appropriately in the `HAVING` clause.¥n¥n4. **Grouping**:¥n   - We need to group the results by `player_id` and `player_name` to calculate the average time on ice for each player.¥n¥n5. **Ordering**:¥n   - Finally, we need to order the results in descending order based on the average time on ice.¥n¥n6. **Constructing the Query**:¥n   - Select the `player_name` and the average `time_on_ice` from the joined tables.¥n   - Use `AVG(pg.time_on_ice)` to calculate the average time on ice.¥n   - Use `HAVING COUNT(pg.game_id) > 10` to filter players who have played more than 10 games.¥n   - Use `HAVING AVG(pg.time_on_ice) > '15:00'` to filter players with an average time on ice greater than 15 minutes.¥n   - Order the results by `average_time_on_ice` in descending order.¥n¥n### Final Query:¥n¥n```sql¥nSELECT ¥n    p.player_name,¥n    AVG(pg.time_on_ice) AS average_time_on_ice¥nFROM ¥n    players p¥nJOIN ¥n    player_game_stats pg ON p.player_id = pg.player_id¥nWHERE ¥n    pg.time_on_ice IS NOT NULL¥nGROUP BY ¥n    p.player_id, p.player_name¥nHAVING ¥n    COUNT(pg.game_id) > 10 AND AVG(pg.time_on_ice) > '15:00'¥nORDER BY ¥n    average_time_on_ice DESC;¥n```¥n¥nThis query will provide the names and average time on ice for players who have played more than 10 games and have an average time on ice greater than 15 minutes, sorted in descending order by their average time on ice.
sql: SELECT ¥n    p.player_name,¥n    AVG(pg.time_on_ice) AS average_time_on_ice¥nFROM ¥n    players p¥nJOIN ¥n    player_game_stats pg ON p.player_id = pg.player_id¥nWHERE ¥n    pg.time_on_ice IS NOT NULL¥nGROUP BY ¥n    p.player_id, p.player_name¥nHAVING ¥n    COUNT(pg.game_id) > 10 AND AVG(pg.time_on_ice) > '15:00'¥nORDER BY ¥n    average_time_on_ice DESC;

question: What are the names of players, along with the dates, whose performance, measured by the change in total points from one game to the next within a specific season, decreased the most?¥n¥n(Note: This question can be further tailored based on the exact needs, but it captures the essence of comparing consecutive game performances within a season.)
cot: To translate the given natural language question into an executable SQLite query, we need to break down the question and understand the required data. The question asks for the names of players along with the dates of their games, where the performance, measured by the change in total points from one game to the next within a specific season, decreased the most.¥n¥nHere's a step-by-step breakdown of how to generate the SQL query:¥n¥n1. **Identify the Required Tables and Columns**:¥n   - We need data from the `players`, `player_game_stats`, and `games` tables.¥n   - From `players`, we need `player_name` and `player_id`.¥n   - From `player_game_stats`, we need `goals`, `assists`, and `game_id`.¥n   - From `games`, we need `date` and `season_id`.¥n¥n2. **Join the Tables**:¥n   - We need to join `players` with `player_game_stats` on `player_id`.¥n   - We then need to join `player_game_stats` with `games` on `game_id`.¥n¥n3. **Calculate Total Points and Performance Change**:¥n   - Calculate total points for each game as `goals + assists`.¥n   - Use the `LEAD` function to get the total points for the next game.¥n   - Calculate the performance change as the difference between the current total points and the next game's total points.¥n¥n4. **Filter and Order the Results**:¥n   - Filter out rows where `next_game_total_points` is `NULL` (i.e., the last game in the season).¥n   - Order the results by `performance_change` in descending order to get the players with the most significant decrease in performance.¥n¥n5. **Limit the Results**:¥n   - If needed, limit the results to the top players with the most significant decrease in performance.¥n¥nHere is the SQL query that accomplishes this:¥n¥n```sql¥nWITH GameStats AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        p.player_name, ¥n        g.game_id, ¥n        g.date,¥n        ps.goals + ps.assists AS total_points,¥n        LEAD(ps.goals + ps.assists) OVER (PARTITION BY p.player_id, g.season_id ORDER BY g.date) AS next_game_total_points¥n    FROM ¥n        players p ¥n        JOIN player_game_stats ps ON p.player_id = ps.player_id¥n        JOIN games g ON ps.game_id = g.game_id¥n    WHERE ¥n        g.season_id = 0  -- Replace 0 with the desired season_id¥n),¥nFilteredStats AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        player_name,¥n        date,¥n        total_points,¥n        next_game_total_points,¥n        (total_points - next_game_total_points) AS performance_change¥n    FROM ¥n        GameStats¥n    WHERE ¥n        next_game_total_points IS NOT NULL¥n)¥n¥nSELECT ¥n    player_name,¥n    date,¥n    performance_change¥nFROM ¥n    FilteredStats¥nORDER BY ¥n    performance_change DESC;¥n```¥n¥n### Explanation of the Query:¥n- **GameStats CTE**: This common table expression calculates the total points for each game and uses the `LEAD` function to get the total points for the next game within the same season for each player.¥n- **FilteredStats CTE**: This CTE filters out rows where `next_game_total_points` is `NULL` and calculates the `performance_change`.¥n- **Final SELECT**: This part selects the player name, date, and performance change, ordering the results by `performance_change` in descending order to show the most significant decreases first.¥n¥n### Notes:¥n- The `season_id` in the `WHERE` clause is set to `0` as a placeholder. Replace `0` with the actual season ID you are interested in.¥n- This query assumes that the `season_id` is known and provided. If the season is not specified, additional logic would be needed to determine the current or desired season.
sql: WITH GameStats AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        p.player_name, ¥n        g.game_id, ¥n        g.date,¥n        ps.goals + ps.assists AS total_points,¥n        LEAD(ps.goals + ps.assists) OVER (PARTITION BY p.player_id, g.season_id ORDER BY g.date) AS next_game_total_points¥n    FROM ¥n        players p ¥n        JOIN player_game_stats ps ON p.player_id = ps.player_id¥n        JOIN games g ON ps.game_id = g.game_id¥n    WHERE ¥n        g.season_id = 0  -- Replace 0 with the desired season_id¥n),¥nFilteredStats AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        player_name,¥n        date,¥n        total_points,¥n        next_game_total_points,¥n        (total_points - next_game_total_points) AS performance_change¥n    FROM ¥n        GameStats¥n    WHERE ¥n        next_game_total_points IS NOT NULL¥n)¥n¥nSELECT ¥n    player_name,¥n    date,¥n    performance_change¥nFROM ¥n    FilteredStats¥nORDER BY ¥n    performance_change DESC;

question: Find the top-performing players in each team across all seasons, ranked by their total goals and assists, and return their names along with their corresponding ranks, ordered by season and team name.
cot: To solve the problem of finding the top-performing players in each team across all seasons, ranked by their total goals and assists, and return their names along with their corresponding ranks, ordered by season and team name, we need to follow these steps:¥n¥n### Step 1: Understand the Requirements¥nWe need to:¥n1. Calculate the total goals and assists for each player in each season.¥n2. Rank players within each team based on their total goals and assists.¥n3. Return the names of these top players along with their ranks.¥n4. Order the results by season and team name.¥n¥n### Step 2: Break Down the Problem¥nWe need to:¥n1. Aggregate player stats by season and team.¥n2. Calculate ranks within each team for the aggregated stats.¥n3. Select the top players from each team.¥n¥n### Step 3: Create SQL Queries¥nWe will use Common Table Expressions (CTEs) to break down the query into manageable parts.¥n¥n#### CTE 1: Aggregate Player Stats¥nWe need to sum the goals and assists for each player in each season.¥n¥n```sql¥nWITH PlayerSeasonStats AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        p.player_id,¥n        p.player_name,¥n        p.team_id,¥n        SUM(pg.goals) as total_goals,¥n        SUM(pg.assists) as total_assists¥n    FROM players p¥n    INNER JOIN player_game_stats pg ON p.player_id = pg.player_id¥n    GROUP BY p.player_id, p.player_name, p.team_id¥n)¥n```¥n¥n#### CTE 2: Rank Players Within Each Team¥nWe need to rank players within each team based on their total goals and assists.¥n¥n```sql¥nWITH RankedPlayers AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        pss.player_id,¥n        pss.player_name,¥n        pss.team_id,¥n        pss.total_goals,¥n        pss.total_assists,¥n        RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY pss.team_id ORDER BY pss.total_goals DESC, pss.total_assists DESC) as player_rank¥n    FROM PlayerSeasonStats pss¥n)¥n```¥n¥n#### CTE 3: Select Top Players¥nWe need to select the top players (rank = 1) from each team.¥n¥n```sql¥nWITH TopPlayers AS (¥n    SELECT ¥n        rp.player_name,¥n        rp.team_id,¥n        rp.player_rank¥n    FROM RankedPlayers rp¥n    WHERE rp.player_rank = 1¥n)¥n```¥n¥n#### Final Query¥nWe need to join the top players with the teams to get the team names and order the results by season and team name.¥n¥n```sql¥nSELECT ¥n    tp.player_name,¥n    tp.player_rank,¥n
